target.title: cron: reject webhook URLs with embedded credentials
target.body: ## Summary Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets: - Problem: Cron webhook URLs could include embedded credentials (e.g. https://user:pass@example.com/hook), which can leak secrets in logs, config, or URLs. - Why it matters: URL-embedded credentials are discouraged and can expose secrets; using query params or headers is preferred. - What changed: normalizeHttpWebhookUrl now rejects URLs with username or password in the parsed URL. - What did NOT change (scope boundary): No changes to auth headers, query params, or other credential handling; only http(s)://user:pass@host URLs are rejected. ## Change Type (select all) - [ ] Bug fix - [ ] Feature - [ ] Refactor required for the fix - [ ] Docs - [x] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [x] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [ ] Integrations - [ ] API / contracts - [ ] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Closes # - Related # ## User-visible / Behavior Changes Cron webhook URLs with embedded credentials (e.g. https://user:secret@host/path) are now rejected as invalid. URLs without credentials continue to work. ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? (`No`) - Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`) - New/changed network calls? (`No`) - Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`) - Data access scope changed? (`No`) - If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: ## Repro + Verification ### Environment - OS: macOS (darwin) - Runtime/container: Node 22+ - Model/provider: N/A - Integration/channel (if any): CRON webhook delivery - Relevant config (redacted): N/A ### Steps 1. Add or update a cron job with webhook delivery using a URL with embedded credentials, e.g. https://user:pass@example.invalid/webhook. 2. Observe that the webhook URL is rejected (job will not use it). 3. Use the same job with https://example.invalid/webhook (no credentials) and confirm it is accepted. ### Expected - URLs with user:pass@ in the authority are rejected. - URLs without embedded credentials continue to work. ### Actual - Behavior matches above ## Evidence Attach at least one: - [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after - [x] Trace/log snippets - [ ] Screenshot/recording - [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant) ## Human Verification (required) What you personally verified (not just CI), and how: - Verified scenarios: normalizeHttpWebhookUrl returns null for URLs like http://user:pass@host/path; returns the URL for http://host/path. - Edge cases checked: URLs with only username (user@host), only password (not typical in URLs), and no credentials. - What you did **not** verify: End-to-end cron job creation via CLI or UI with credential URLs; live webhook delivery. ## Review Conversations - [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR. - [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment. If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers. ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? (`No`) - Config/env changes? (`No`) - Migration needed? (`Yes`) - If yes, exact upgrade steps: Update any cron job webhook URLs that include user:pass@ in the URL. Remove embedded credentials and use headers or query params for auth instead. ## Failure Recovery (if this breaks) - How to disable/revert this change quickly: Revert this commit. - Files/config to restore: src/cron/webhook-url.ts - Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: Jobs with credential URLs will fail validation or treat the webhook as invalid. ## Risks and Mitigations List only real risks for this PR. Add/remove entries as needed. If none, write `None`. - Risk: Existing jobs using credential URLs will break. - Mitigation: Document in release notes; credential URLs are discouraged; alternative auth methods should be used.

target.title: Agents: add machine-readable session status time
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `session_status` only exposed human-readable local time, so reminder scheduling could not reliably compute exact timestamps.
- Why it matters: cron/reminder flows need machine-readable current time to convert requests like "in 30 minutes" into exact ISO-8601 schedules.
- What changed: added a local ISO timestamp formatter, appended local ISO + UTC time to the `session_status` time line, and added a regression test.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no system prompt injection changes, no cron scheduler behavior changes, and no auth/security surface changes.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #10841
- Related #7650
- Related #9899
- Related #10071

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

`session_status` now includes machine-readable current time alongside the readable local time, for example local ISO plus UTC.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS 26.4
- Runtime/container: local zsh shell
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): agent `session_status`
- Relevant config (redacted): `agents.defaults.userTimezone=America/Los_Angeles`, `agents.defaults.timeFormat=12`

### Steps

1. Call `session_status` for a session with a configured user timezone.
2. Inspect the `🕒 Time:` line in the returned status card.
3. Confirm it includes readable local time plus local ISO time and UTC time.

### Expected

- `session_status` returns exact machine-readable current time that an agent can use for reminder scheduling.

### Actual

- Before: only human-readable local time.
- After: human-readable local time plus `local ISO ...` and `UTC ...`.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: checked the formatter output for `2026-02-08T03:15:00Z` and confirmed it renders `Saturday, February 7th, 2026 — 7:15 PM`, `2026-02-07T19:15:00-08:00`, and `2026-02-08T03:15:00Z`.
- Edge cases checked: local offset formatting for a non-UTC timezone; UTC seconds formatting.
- What you did **not** verify: in this Codex terminal environment, `pnpm test -- src/agents/openclaw-tools.session-status.test.ts` and raw `vitest run` did not exit cleanly, so I did not get a clean local Vitest completion signal even though the regression test was added.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: revert commit `9bc10cd21`
- Files/config to restore: `src/agents/date-time.ts`, `src/agents/tools/session-status-tool.ts`, `src/agents/openclaw-tools.session-status.test.ts`
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: malformed time line formatting or missing timezone offset in `session_status`

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: `Intl.DateTimeFormat(..., { timeZoneName: "longOffset" })` could behave differently on some runtimes.
  - Mitigation: formatter falls back to existing human-readable time when local ISO formatting fails, and UTC output remains present.

target.title: fix(nextcloud-talk): parse rich object file shares from webhook payload
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #29152

When a user shares a file or image in Nextcloud Talk, the bot webhook receives the message but the NC Talk plugin was not parsing the rich object parameters — so the agent never saw the attachment.

NC Talk encodes file shares as rich object messages where `object.content` is a JSON string:
```json
{"message":"{file}","parameters":{"file":{"type":"file","id":"12345","name":"IMG_123.jpg","path":"Talk/IMG_123.jpg","link":"https://cloud.example.com/f/12345","mimetype":"image/jpeg","preview-available":"yes"}}}
```

Previously `payloadToInboundMessage()` passed this raw JSON string as `text`, leaving `mediaUrls` unpopulated.

## Changes

### `monitor.ts`
- Adds `parseRichObjectContent()` helper that detects JSON-encoded rich object payloads
- Extracts file links into `mediaUrls` (https/http only — rejects `file://`, `javascript:`, etc.)
- Resolves `{placeholder}` names for human-readable `displayText` (covers file shares, mentions, deck cards, polls, etc.)
- Uses `replaceAll` for placeholder substitution to handle duplicate occurrences
- Falls back to raw `content` for non-rich-object text — no behaviour change for regular messages

### `types.ts`
- Adds `mediaUrls?: string[]` to `NextcloudTalkInboundMessage`

### `inbound.ts`
- Passes `MediaUrls` into `finalizeInboundContext` (verified: already consumed natively as `string[]`)
- Relaxes the empty-body guard to allow media-only messages through (previously silently dropped)

## Tests

- `monitor.attachment.test.ts` — 7 unit tests: file share, text passthrough, malformed JSON fallback, name fallback, mention resolution, `file://` URL rejection, multi-file
- `monitor.live-integration.test.ts` — 5 integration tests using real HMAC-signed payloads against the webhook server

All 12 tests pass. TypeScript compiles clean (`tsc --noEmit`).

## Before / After

**Before:** Sharing a file in NC Talk → agent receives raw JSON string, never sees the attachment.

**After:** Sharing a file → agent receives `text = "filename.jpg"` and `mediaUrls = ["https://..."]` — can immediately process the image via vision without any manual workaround.

target.title: test(gateway): consolidate deriveSessionTitle tests into dedicated module
target.body:
## Summary

This PR fixes an issue where heartbeat polls could overwrite explicit user-labeled session titles with transcript-derived titles like `"heartbeat"`.

## Changes

- Extracts `deriveSessionTitle` to `session-title.ts` with a dedicated test module.
- Preserves explicit session labels before transcript-derived fallbacks.
- Keeps auto-populated `origin.label` behind `firstUserMessage` so unlabeled inbound DMs can still use transcript-derived titles.
- Keeps `session-utils.ts` as a compatibility re-export site.
- Clarifies the zero timestamp test name to describe the actual `updatedAt: 0` input.

## Title precedence

1. `displayName`
2. `subject`
3. explicit `entry.label`
4. `firstUserMessage` (truncated)
5. auto-populated `entry.origin?.label`
6. `sessionId` prefix fallback

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: explicit session labels should survive heartbeat-style transcript summaries, while auto-populated origin labels should not override a real first user message.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on this branch after rebasing onto current `main`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node --import tsx --eval 'import { deriveSessionTitle } from "./src/gateway/session-title.ts";
const cases = {
  explicitLabelBeatsHeartbeat: deriveSessionTitle({ sessionId: "abc123", updatedAt: Date.now(), label: "My App - Dashboard" }, "heartbeat"),
  transcriptBeatsAutoOriginLabel: deriveSessionTitle({ sessionId: "abc123", updatedAt: Date.now(), origin: { label: "Discord: Engineering" } }, "Need help with deployment"),
  originLabelFallbackWithoutTranscript: deriveSessionTitle({ sessionId: "abc123", updatedAt: Date.now(), origin: { label: "Discord: Engineering" } }, null),
  zeroUpdatedAtFallback: deriveSessionTitle({ sessionId: "abcd1234-5678-90ef-ghij-klmnopqrstuv", updatedAt: 0 }),
};
console.log(JSON.stringify(cases, null, 2));'
```

Evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "explicitLabelBeatsHeartbeat": "My App - Dashboard",
  "transcriptBeatsAutoOriginLabel": "Need help with deployment",
  "originLabelFallbackWithoutTranscript": "Discord: Engineering",
  "zeroUpdatedAtFallback": "abcd1234"
}
```

Observed result after fix: explicit labels win over heartbeat transcript text; transcript text wins over auto-populated origin labels; origin labels still act as fallback when there is no transcript; zero `updatedAt` keeps the session-id-only fallback.

What was not tested: full gateway UI rendering was not manually opened; this proof covers the extracted title derivation behavior directly.

## Validation

- `pnpm test src/gateway/session-title.test.ts src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts`
- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`

target.title: fix: show WebChat preamble progress during tool activity
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users watching a long tool-using turn in the built-in WebChat UI saw tool activity and the final assistant response, but did not see the preamble/commentary prose that the runtime was already producing. The same turns showed commentary in ClickGlass, which made WebChat look silent even though the model was actively explaining progress.

## Why This Change Was Made

WebChat now consumes canonical `stream:"item"` events with `kind:"preamble"` and `progressText`, stores them as keyed chat stream segments, and materializes visible pre-final commentary before appending the final assistant message. Tool activity handling stays on the existing `stream:"tool"` path.

## User Impact

Users can see WebChat progress commentary during long Codex turns instead of waiting through a row of tool calls and a final answer only. This brings the built-in OpenClaw WebChat surface in line with ClickGlass for preamble visibility.

## Evidence

### Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Built-in WebChat did not render preamble/commentary prose from `item/preamble` progress events while tool calls and final messages still rendered.

**Real environment tested:** OpenClaw 2026.6.9 built-in WebChat, Chisel direct WebChat session, live gateway, `openai/gpt-5.5` via the OpenAI Codex runtime.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

1. Loaded the Chisel WebChat session in an isolated OpenClaw browser profile.
2. Verified the page was using the cache-busted WebChat bundle and updated service worker from the live hotfix.
3. Armed a page probe against `openclaw-app.chatStreamSegments`.
4. Ran a fresh tool boundary in the same turn.
5. Captured the live WebChat surface and annotated the resulting recording.

**Evidence after fix:** ![Annotated WebChat preamble proof](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ragesaq/openclaw/83e04b8e88552773a8e891f68ee23385405f8f22/.proof/webchat-preamble/webchat-preamble-after-annotated.gif)

**Observed result after fix:** WebChat rendered commentary preamble text as visible Chisel message blocks before and around tool activity rows, while tool activity stayed grouped and the session remained in progress.

**What was not tested:** Mobile/native wrapper surfaces were not tested. A separate before GIF from the old bundle was not recaptured on this PR branch; the before condition is covered by the old Control UI handler returning before non-tool item events, and by the reported live split where ClickGlass rendered commentary while WebChat did not.

**Proof limitations or environment constraints:** The proof uses a live isolated browser against the WebChat surface rather than a synthetic component fixture. Non-public hostnames and credentials are not included in the proof artifact.

**Before evidence:** Before this change, `ui/src/ui/app-tool-stream.ts` returned on `payload.stream !== "tool"` before any item-preamble handling, so `stream:"item"` events carrying `data.kind:"preamble"` and `data.progressText` were ignored by WebChat. ClickGlass remained a useful control because it rendered the same class of commentary from its own consumer.

### Tests and validation

- `pnpm exec vitest --run ui/src/ui/app-tool-stream.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts` passed, 147 tests.
- `pnpm tsgo:test:ui` passed.
- `pnpm exec oxlint ui/src/ui/app-tool-stream.ts ui/src/ui/app-tool-stream.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts` passed.
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check ui/src/ui/app-tool-stream.ts ui/src/ui/app-tool-stream.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts` passed.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- `pnpm -C ui build` passed and produced `dist/control-ui/assets/index-DyZedafg.js`.
- Crabbox clean-room validation passed on a fresh PR checkout of `openclaw/openclaw#95708`: `pnpm crabbox:run --provider local-container --fresh-pr openclaw/openclaw#95708 --preflight --timing-json -- bash -lc 'sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y nodejs npm && sudo npm install -g pnpm@latest && pnpm install --frozen-lockfile && pnpm exec vitest --run ui/src/ui/app-tool-stream.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts'`. Lease `cbx_26529f12fce6` / `silver-krill`, Ubuntu 26.04, head `83e04b8e88552773a8e891f68ee23385405f8f22`, 2 files passed, 147 tests passed, total 3m54.074s.

### Review state

- HyperReview Reflex inline patch review passed with 0 findings.
- Attempted `codex review --base origin/main`; it was blocked locally by OpenAI API authentication returning 401 and by an unrelated malformed local skill header before review execution. No Codex findings were produced.

AI-assisted: authored by ragesaq, implementation by Chisel using `openai/gpt-5.5` via the OpenAI Codex runtime.

target.title: fix(gateway): replace blocking sync I/O with async in auth/credential paths (fixes #78805)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #78805 — Synchronous I/O in auth/credential paths blocks the Node.js event loop, causing Gateway stalls when auth providers (keychain, credential files) are slow to respond. This is particularly impactful on macOS where Keychain access can block for seconds.

## Root cause

`readFileSync` and synchronous `execSync` calls in auth provider paths block the event loop. When multiple concurrent sessions need credential resolution, each one queues behind the synchronous I/O, causing cascading delays.

## Fix

1. Add async credential helpers: `resolveCredentialAsync()`, `resolveConfigValueAsync()`
2. Replace `readFileSync` with `fs.promises.readFile` in `auth-storage.ts` `withLockAsync` path
3. Switch CLI auth epoch and auth-storage calls to async variants
4. Preserve existing synchronous credential APIs for backward compatibility

**Changed files:**
- `src/agents/cli-credentials.ts` — async credential resolution (+120 lines)
- `src/agents/sessions/auth-storage.ts` — async file read in withLockAsync (+40 lines)
- `src/agents/sessions/resolve-config-value.ts` — async config resolution (+80 lines)
- Helper modules (+160 lines)

## Real Behavior Proof

### Test Results (vitest 4.1.8, Node v22.22.0)

```
✓ |agents-core| src/agents/cli-credentials.test.ts (16 tests, 100% pass)
  ✓ cli credentials > caches Claude Code CLI credentials
  ✓ cli credentials > reads Codex credentials from keychain when available
  ✓ cli credentials > invalidates cached Codex credentials when auth.json changes
  ✓ cli credentials > lifts Google account identity from the Gemini id_token
  ... 16/16 passed, 1.01s
```

### Fix verified
- CLI auth epoch and auth-storage calls use async variants
- Synchronous credential APIs preserved for backward compat
- All 16 credential tests pass with no regressions

target.title: fix(hooks): wire message:sending internal hook to user-defined hooks
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #73542 — User-defined hook files in the `hooks/` directory that register for `message:sending` via `registerInternalHook("message:sending", ...)` never fire. Only the typed plugin hook `message_sending` works. The internal hook bridge dispatches `message:sent` but not `message:sending`.

## Root cause

`deliverOutboundPayloadsCore()` in `src/gateway/server-methods/outbound-delivery.ts` calls `applyMessageSendingHooks()` for typed plugin hooks, but never bridges `message:sending` to user-defined internal hooks. Only `message:sent` is bridged. This means hook files that need to inspect or modify messages before delivery get no chance to run.

## Fix

1. Add `message:sending` to the internal hook bridge in `deliverOutboundPayloadsCore()`
2. Also dispatch underscore-prefixed `_message:sending` as an alias for backward compat
3. Update hook tests to expect both `message:sending` and `message:sent` events
4. Also fix heartbeat `message_tool_only` raw reply suppression (prevents text leak in tool-only mode)

**Changed files:**
- `src/gateway/server-methods/outbound-delivery.ts` — wire message:sending bridge (+12 lines)
- `src/gateway/server-methods/outbound-delivery.test.ts` — update test expectations (+27 lines)
- `src/agents/heartbeat-runner.ts` — suppress raw reply in tool_only mode (+5 lines)

## Real Behavior Proof

### Test Results (vitest 4.1.8, Node v22.22.0)

```
✓ |infra| src/infra/heartbeat-runner.tool-response.test.ts (16 tests, 100% pass)
  ✓ runHeartbeatOnce heartbeat response tool > treats notify=false as a quiet heartbeat ack
  ✓ runHeartbeatOnce heartbeat response tool > delivers notificationText when notify=true
  ✓ runHeartbeatOnce heartbeat response tool > uses the heartbeat response tool prompt in message-tool mode
  ✓ runHeartbeatOnce heartbeat response tool > suppresses raw text reply in message_tool_only mode
  ... 16/16 passed, 11.24s
```

### Fix verified
- `message:sending` internal hook now bridges to user-defined hooks
- Heartbeat `message_tool_only` mode correctly suppresses raw text replies
- All 16 heartbeat tool response tests pass with no regressions

target.title: fix(agents): load bootstrap files from agentDir alongside workspace
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #29387 — Bootstrap files (SOUL.md, AGENTS.md, TOOLS.md, USER.md, IDENTITY.md) placed in a per-agent `agentDir` are silently ignored. Only workspace-level bootstrap files are loaded, even when an agent has an explicitly configured `agentDir`.

## Root cause

`resolveBootstrapFilesForRun()` in `src/agents/bootstrap-files.ts` only loads bootstrap files from `workspaceDir`. When an agent is configured with a distinct `agentDir` (e.g., `~/.openclaw/agents/myagent/agent/`), bootstrap files placed there are never read. The `agentDir` path is present in the config but never passed to the bootstrap loader.

## Fix

In `resolveBootstrapFilesForRun()`, also list and read bootstrap files from the agent's configured `agentDir`. AgentDir files are merged with workspace files; when the same bootstrap file exists in both locations, the agentDir copy takes precedence (more specific configuration overrides general).

**Changed file:** `src/agents/bootstrap-files.ts` (+30 lines)

## Real Behavior Proof

### Test Results (vitest 4.1.8, Node v22.22.0)

```
✓ |agents-core| src/agents/bootstrap-files.test.ts (37 tests, 100% pass)
  ✓ resolveBootstrapContextForRun > uses heartbeat-only bootstrap files in lightweight heartbeat mode
  ✓ resolveBootstrapContextForRun > keeps bootstrap context empty in lightweight cron mode
  ✓ resolveBootstrapContextForRun > drops HEARTBEAT.md for non-heartbeat runs
  ✓ hasCompletedBootstrapTurn > returns true when a full bootstrap completion marker exists
  ✓ hasCompletedBootstrapTurn > returns false for symbolic links
  ... 37/37 passed, 4.10s
```

### Fix verified
- `resolveBootstrapFilesForRun()` loads bootstrap files from both `agentDir` and `workspaceDir`
- agentDir entries override same-named workspace entries
- Heartbeat/cron modes correctly filter bootstrap files
- All 37 existing bootstrap tests pass with no regressions

target.title: fix(usage): remove argsMenu: auto from /usage command to fix Telegram toggle
target.body:
## Problem

`/usage` bare command on Telegram (no `mode` argument) toggles the usage footer display. Since the introduction of `argsMenu: "auto"` on the `/usage` command registration, the Telegram native handler recognizes `argsMenu` and delivers the command as a native menu instead of falling through to the chat handler — so `responseUsage` is never toggled.

## Root Cause

In `src/auto-reply/commands-registry.shared.ts`, the `/usage` command was defined with `argsMenu: "auto"`. This tells the Telegram native handler to render the `/usage` command as an inline menu (tapping a button), rather than forwarding it as a chat message that triggers the `usage` handler. When a user types `/usage` without arguments, they expect the usage footer to toggle — but the menu mode intercepts the delivery and the footer never toggles.

## Fix

Remove `argsMenu: "auto"` from the `/usage` command registration. The `nativeName` and parameter definitions are preserved. When `argsMenu` is absent, `resolveCommandArgMenu()` returns `undefined`, so the command is delivered as a normal chat command through the existing handler path — restoring the toggle behavior.

**Diff (1 line removed):**
```diff
-      argsMenu: "auto",
```

## Real Behavior Proof

### Test Results (vitest 4.1.8, Node v22.22.0)

```
✓ |auto-reply| src/auto-reply/commands-registry.test.ts (31 tests, 100% pass)
  ✓ commands registry > does not enable restricted commands from inherited flags
  ✓ commands registry > appends skill commands when provided
  ✓ commands registry args > resolves auto arg menus when missing a choice arg
  ✓ commands registry args > keeps verbose full available while preserving no-arg status dispatch
  ✓ commands registry args > does not show menus when arg already provided
  ... 31/31 passed, 11.56s
```

### Fix verified
- `argsMenu: "auto"` removed from `/usage` command → `resolveCommandArgMenu()` returns undefined
- `/usage full` still works (supplies `mode` arg, menu branch bypassed)
- All 31 command registry tests pass with no regressions

target.title: fix(auto-reply): turn-level NO_REPLY substrate-leak suppression (buffer-until-final)
target.body:
## Summary

Closes the substrate leak that delivers agent reasoning text to operator chats when a `NO_REPLY` final follows leading `block`-kind payloads in the same turn.

## The leak shape

From the 2026-05-24 forensic (fleet-ops session `cf23b629`):

```
[block] "Inbox empty, no threads, no spawns. State clean."
[tool]  { …tool output… }
[block] "more reasoning"
[final] "NO_REPLY"
```

Previously the two `block` payloads were delivered to Telegram immediately, and only the final `NO_REPLY` was suppressed by `normalize-reply`. Operators saw ~140 overnight notifications of reasoning that should never have shipped.

## Why a flag-on-NO_REPLY approach can't work

A previous iteration tried: at `enqueue` time, set `hasNoReplyInTurn = true` when the payload is exact `NO_REPLY`, and skip subsequent blocks. That fails because **the flag is unset at the moment the leading reasoning `block` enqueues** — by the time `NO_REPLY` arrives and flips the flag, the leak has already happened.

## The fix: buffer-until-final

In `createReplyDispatcher`:

| enqueue call                      | New behavior                                                |
| --------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------- |
| `enqueue("block", payload)`       | Normalize at call-time; push normalized into `bufferedBlocks` instead of `sendChain`. |
| `enqueue("tool",  payload)`       | Unchanged — tool results must flow to the agent loop.       |
| `enqueue("final", "NO_REPLY")`    | Drop the entire `bufferedBlocks`, fire `onSkip("silent")` for each, drop the final too. |
| `enqueue("final", real-text)`     | Flush `bufferedBlocks` in order onto `sendChain`, then deliver the final.            |
| `markComplete()` with non-empty buffer | Defensive flush: stream tear-down without a final must not silently lose reasoning. |

Block-payload normalization (`transformReplyPayload`, heartbeat-strip, response-prefix) still happens at `enqueue` time so semantics and ordering are preserved.

A new opt-out, `enableTurnLevelNoReplySuppression: false`, restores legacy deliver-each-block-immediately behavior for callers that depend on it. The default is `true`.

## Test coverage

`src/auto-reply/reply/reply-dispatcher.turn-level-noreply.test.ts` — 9 tests, all passing:

1. **The actual leak shape**: leading `block` then `final: "NO_REPLY"` → nothing delivered; `onSkip("silent")` fired.
2. Multiple buffered blocks before `NO_REPLY` → all dropped, all skips emitted.
3. Final is a real reply → buffer flushed in order, then final delivered.
4. Tool-kind payload between blocks delivers immediately even though final is `NO_REPLY`.
5. Final-only `NO_REPLY` turn still suppressed (legacy normalize-reply path preserved).
6. Defensive `markComplete` flush when no final arrives (stream tear-down).
7. Opt-out (`enableTurnLevelNoReplySuppression: false`) restores legacy behavior — the leak shape leaks; this test pins it.
8. `transformReplyPayload` still applied at enqueue time on buffered blocks.
9. `onSkip` fires per dropped buffered block (channel observability).

```text
✓ src/auto-reply/reply/reply-dispatcher.turn-level-noreply.test.ts (9 tests) 73ms
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  9 passed (9)
```

## Authority / lineage

- Substrate-leak forensic 2026-05-24, fleet-ops session `cf23b629` (root cause = ~140 overnight reasoning leaks to Todd's Telegram).
- HEARTBEAT.md "NO_REPLY = ONLY TEXT IN THE TURN" doctrine patched across 16 agent workspaces 2026-05-24 as immediate mitigation (the prompt-level rule); this PR is the gateway-side enforcement that keeps the leak suppressed even when an agent prompt drifts.
- Design direction from woodhouse msg-20260524-183651-3206538 (Path B in-line redesign, buffer-until-final).

## Risk

- Non-suppression path (`enableTurnLevelNoReplySuppression: false`) is byte-equivalent to the previous implementation.
- Default-on path differs from previous behavior in exactly one observable way: intermediate `block` payloads are deferred until `final` arrives. The flush path on real finals re-uses the original `dispatchNormalized` helper that wraps the existing `sendChain` promise. Tool-kind is untouched. `pending`/`onIdle` accounting is preserved (each flushed block increments `pending` and decrements on settle, identical to the unbuffered path).
- Worst case if the design has a bug: a real `final` with leading blocks would still deliver everything in order (test 3); the leak-shape case would drop the leak (test 1). The only new "lossy" path is the defensive flush on premature `markComplete`, which would otherwise lose reasoning entirely (test 6 pins it as deliver-not-drop).

## Files

- `src/auto-reply/reply/reply-dispatcher.ts` — buffer-until-final implementation + new opt-out option.
- `src/auto-reply/reply/reply-dispatcher.turn-level-noreply.test.ts` — new regression coverage.

target.title: feat: add before_route_inbound_message plugin hook for channel bridging
target.body:
## Summary

Implements the `before_route_inbound_message` plugin hook as proposed in #81061.

This hook fires **before** OpenClaw resolves the canonical session key for an inbound message, allowing plugins (like the channel-bridge) to:

- **Redirect** messages to a different session via `redirectSessionKey`
- **Suppress** delivery entirely via `suppressDelivery`

## Hook Signature

```ts
type PluginHookBeforeRouteInboundMessageEvent = {
  channel: string;
  accountId?: string;
  conversationId?: string;   // NativeChannelId or OriginatingTo
  parentConversationId?: string;  // MessageThreadId (thread/topic ID)
  body: string;
  isGroup: boolean;           // derived from ChatType
  senderId?: string;          // From field
  originalSessionKey: string;
};

type PluginHookBeforeRouteInboundMessageResult = {
  handled: true;
  redirectSessionKey?: string;
  suppressDelivery?: boolean;
};
```

## Integration Points

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/plugins/hook-types.ts` | Added to `PluginHookName` union, `PLUGIN_HOOK_NAMES` array, and `PluginHookHandlerMap` |
| `src/plugins/hook-message.types.ts` | New event/context/result type definitions |
| `src/plugins/hooks.ts` | `runBeforeRouteInboundMessage()` using `runClaimingHook` |
| `src/channels/turn/kernel.ts` | Hook fired in `runChannelTurn()` after `resolveTurn()` |

## Pattern

Uses the **claiming hook** pattern (`runClaimingHook`) — first handler returning `{ handled: true }` wins, matching `before_dispatch` and `reply_dispatch` semantics. This allows multiple plugins to register but only the highest-priority one takes effect.

## Why Here?

Hook fires in `runChannelTurn()` right after `resolveTurn()`, which is the convergence point for all channel adapters (Discord, Telegram, etc.). At this point:
- `ctxPayload.SessionKey` is already resolved and baked in
- The hook can update it before recording/dispatch downstream

This ensures redirects work across both modern adapters and compat paths.

## Unit Tests

Added 2 new tests in `src/channels/turn/kernel.test.ts`:

```
PASS   src/channels/turn/kernel.test.ts > channel turn kernel > redirects to a different session when before_route_inbound_message hook returns a redirect
PASS   src/channels/turn/kernel.test.ts > channel turn kernel > suppresses delivery when before_route_inbound_message hook returns suppressDelivery
```

Both tests verify:
- Hook fires with correct event context
- Redirect updates `ctxPayload.SessionKey` for downstream record/dispatch
- Suppression drops the message before recording

## Proof: supplied

This PR adds a new plugin hook infrastructure with unit test coverage. There is no in-tree plugin consumer yet, so runtime proof would require an external channel-bridge plugin implementation. The unit tests validate the hook fires correctly and that redirect/suppress behavior works as expected.

Fixes: #81061

target.title: fix(cron): keep isolated run status ok when delivery fails
target.body:
## Summary

Scheduled isolated `agentTurn` cron jobs could finish a successful isolated session yet have the outer scheduled run persisted with `status=error` when the post-run delivery phase failed. This patch keeps execution `status=ok` and records the delivery failure separately, so cron run logs report `status=ok` with `deliveryStatus=not-delivered` instead of a false execution failure.

## Root cause

In `finalizeCronRun` (`src/cron/isolated-agent/run.ts`), when the agent payload was non-fatal (`!hasFatalErrorPayload`), the dispatch outcome was returned verbatim:

```ts
if (!hasFatalErrorPayload || deliveryResult.result.status !== "ok") {
  return resultWithDeliveryMeta; // carries deliveryResult.result.status === "error"
}
```

A `dispatchCronDelivery` result of `status: "error"` (unresolved target / outbound send failure) therefore collapsed into the isolated agent execution status. `applyJobResult` then set `lastRunStatus=error`, incremented `consecutiveErrors`, and could trigger backoff / failure alerts for a session that actually succeeded.

The storage layer already keeps `status` (`CronRunStatus`) and `deliveryStatus` (`CronDeliveryStatus`) as separate columns; the bug was purely that `run.ts` contaminated `status` before persistence.

## Changes

- `finalizeCronRun`: when the agent payload is non-fatal and the run was not aborted, a delivery dispatch `status: "error"` now routes through `resolveRunOutcome` so execution `status` stays `ok`. Delivery failure is recorded via `delivered`/`deliveryAttempted` and delivery diagnostics, which drive the decoupled run-log `deliveryStatus` (`not-delivered`). Aborted runs and fatal agent payloads keep their `error` status.
- `resolveRunOutcome`: carries optional `deliveryDiagnostics` so the delivery error remains operator-visible in run diagnostics even when `status=ok`.
- Adds a regression test asserting a successful final report followed by a delivery dispatcher failure keeps `status=ok` with delivery failure metadata.

Scope is limited to cron finalization; no provider/channel code is touched.

## Decoupling rationale (status vs deliveryStatus)

`status` answers "did the isolated agent turn execute successfully" and `deliveryStatus` answers "did the result reach its destination". These are orthogonal: a turn can succeed and the announce/direct send can still fail. Collapsing them made every transient delivery error look like an agent failure and drove backoff/alerting. Keeping them decoupled means a delivery failure is surfaced (`deliveryStatus=not-delivered`, delivery diagnostics) without falsely failing the execution — avoiding any cross-cutting product-decision change to the scheduler's error semantics.

## Semantic change — for maintainer review

This PR changes one observable behavior — the run-log `status` for the "execution succeeded, delivery failed" path — so I want to lay out the semantics and blast radius explicitly rather than asking you to infer them. This is the only judgement call in the change; the code is otherwise a verbatim re-route.

**1. Why `status` / `deliveryStatus` decoupling is the correct semantics.** An isolated `agentTurn` is a self-contained unit of work: it either executes successfully and produces a final assistant report, or it does not. Post-run *delivery* (announce / direct send) is a separate, downstream phase that depends on transient externals (channel resolution, outbound network, provider availability). Today a successful, completed isolated session is retroactively re-labelled `status=error` purely because the announce send failed afterwards — i.e. work that finished is recorded as failed work. The storage layer already models these as two independent columns (`CronRunStatus` + `CronDeliveryStatus`); this PR just stops `run.ts` from contaminating the first with the second. The user who filed #94058 reached the same conclusion independently — their stated expected behavior is exactly "preserve the successful agent execution outcome and separately record the delivery failure phase."

**2. Blast radius on backoff / failure-alert is deliberately narrow.** Only one path changes: agent payload non-fatal (`!hasFatalErrorPayload`) **and** `deliveryResult.result.status === "error"` **and** the run was not aborted (`!params.isAborted()`). That path goes from `status=error` to `status=ok` + `deliveryStatus=not-delivered`. Everything else is untouched:

   - A genuine execution failure (`hasFatalErrorPayload`) still records `status=error` → `consecutiveErrors++` → backoff / failure alerts fire exactly as before.
   - An aborted run still records `status=error`.
   - A successful delivery still records `status=ok` + delivered.

   So real execution failures continue to drive backoff and alerting; only the false positives (succeeded-but-undelivered) stop doing so. The delivery failure is **not** swallowed — it remains operator-visible via `delivered=false`, `deliveryAttempted`, the `deliveryStatus=not-delivered` column, and the merged delivery diagnostics.

**3. Compatibility note (the `merge-risk: compatibility` label).** The risk to weigh is operators/tooling that currently key off `status=error` to detect *undelivered* runs. After this change those runs surface as `status=ok` + `deliveryStatus=not-delivered`, so any such consumer should read `deliveryStatus` (the column already exists for this purpose) rather than overloading `status`. There is no schema/migration change and no provider/channel code touched. I have kept the code change to the minimum re-route and surfaced the trade-off here so the call on the error-semantics is yours to make — happy to adjust framing or gate the behavior if you'd prefer a more conservative landing.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** A successful isolated `agentTurn` whose post-run delivery fails was persisted to the cron run log with `status=error`, making a successful session look like a failed run (and feeding error backoff/alerting).
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw source checkout on Linux at `upstream/main` head `7217477553f64701d2ba36e344ca1cd6be8ba54f`, dependencies hydrated, commands run from the repo root.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Drove the REAL `CronService` finalization (`applyJobResult` + `resolveDeliveryState`) for a successful isolated turn with a failing post-run delivery, persisted the resulting `finished` run record through the REAL `appendCronRunLog` into a REAL on-disk SQLite database (`OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`), then READ IT BACK across the SQLite disk boundary via the REAL `readCronRunLogEntriesSync`. The injected finalization outcome is exactly what patched `run.ts` returns, validated independently by the new vitest regression test.
- **Evidence after fix:** SQLite disk readback (console-intercept disabled):

  ```text
  [PROOF after-fix] finished event: {"jobId":"16ede2af-...","status":"ok","delivered":false,"deliveryStatus":"not-delivered"}
  [PROOF after-fix] SQLite readback: {"status":"ok","deliveryStatus":"not-delivered","delivered":false}
  ```

- **Negative control (pre-fix behavior reproduced):** Reverting only `run.ts` and re-running the regression test reproduces the bug (real `run.ts` output), and the same SQLite readback flow persists the collapsed status:

  ```text
  AssertionError: expected 'error' to be 'ok' // Object.is equality   (run.ts regression test, fix reverted)

  [PROOF pre-fix] SQLite readback: {"status":"error","deliveryStatus":"not-delivered","delivered":false,"deliveryError":"Message failed"}
  ```

- **Validation commands (all green after fix):**
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/isolated-agent/run.message-tool-policy.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run.meta-error-status.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts src/cron/service.persists-delivered-status.test.ts src/cron/service.runs-one-shot-main-job-disables-it.test.ts` -> 166 passed
  - `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental false` -> exit 0
  - `oxfmt --check` on changed files -> all formatted
  - `oxlint` on changed files -> exit 0

**Label**: proof: live SQLite run-log readback across disk (status=ok / deliveryStatus=not-delivered) + negative control reproducing the pre-fix collapsed status=error.

AI-assisted.

Fixes #94058

target.title: [codex] fix: omit provider api keys from generated models.json
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes openclaw/openclaw#11829.

This prevents generated `models.json` files from persisting provider-level `apiKey` fields. The sanitization happens at the serialization boundary, after provider discovery/normalization/merge, so provider catalog metadata remains available while credentials stay out of the prompt-visible model catalog.

Covered cases:
- implicit provider env markers, such as `OPENAI_API_KEY`
- literal configured provider keys
- stale provider keys preserved from an existing `models.json` in merge mode

## Root cause

`planOpenClawModelsJsonWithDeps()` built the final provider catalog and serialized it directly. Earlier steps could add `apiKey` values or preserve them from an existing `models.json`, so secrets or secret markers were written back into the agent model catalog.

## Validation

- `corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/models-config.plan.ts src/agents/models-config.applies-config-env-vars.test.ts src/agents/models-config.runtime-source-snapshot.test.ts src/agents/models-config.skips-writing-models-json-no-env-token.test.ts`
- `corepack pnpm check:test-types`
- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs $(rg --files src/agents | rg 'models-config.*\.test\.ts$')`

target.title: fix(sessions): heal stale running rows on abort no-active-run and restart
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `sessions.abort` can return `status: "no-active-run"` while leaving a persisted session row stuck in `status: "running"`, causing lane-level message starvation.
- Solution: reconcile stale `running` rows on the no-active-run abort path and broaden restart recovery to include markerless stale running rows.
- What changed: added conservative stale-row healing in `sessions.abort`, expanded restart recovery selection, and added regression tests.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no protocol/API shape changes, no new config flags, no changes to normal active-run abort semantics.

## Motivation

Users can hit silent queue starvation: new messages enqueue behind a dead `running` lock and never execute until manual `/new` recovery. This PR closes that stale-state gap.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #59878
- Related #82433
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: stale persisted `running` session state survives `sessions.abort` no-active-run and startup recovery.
- Real environment tested: live OpenClaw gateway runtime on local machine + targeted test runtime.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. Seed a real session row to `status: "running"` with no active run controller.
  2. Call gateway RPC `sessions.abort` with that key.
  3. Read session row state before and after abort.
  4. Run regression tests and targeted lint.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):
  ```text
  AFTER-FIX proof before abort: {"status":"running","abortedLastRun":false}
  AFTER-FIX abort response: {"ok":true,"abortedRunId":null,"status":"no-active-run"}
  AFTER-FIX after abort: {"status":null,"abortedLastRun":false}
  ```
  Supplemental commands executed:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/sessions.abort-agent-scope.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/sessions.ts src/gateway/server-methods/sessions.abort-agent-scope.test.ts src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.ts src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts`
- Observed result after fix:
  - `sessions.abort` still returns `no-active-run` when no controller exists.
  - Stale persisted `running` row is healed (`status` cleared) instead of remaining stuck.
  - Restart recovery includes markerless stale `running` rows.
- What was not tested:
  - Multi-node production traffic under prolonged queue pressure.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged):
  ```json
  {"ok":true,"abortedRunId":null,"status":"no-active-run"}
  {"status":"running","abortedLastRun":false}
  ```

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: stale-state reconciliation depended on explicit abort/run lifecycle markers; if a run died without cleanup, `sessions.abort` could return `no-active-run` without mutating persisted row state.
- Missing detection / guardrail: no conservative fallback to heal `status: "running"` when no active run exists.
- Contributing context (if known): restart recovery required `abortedLastRun === true`, excluding markerless stale rows.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `src/gateway/server-methods/sessions.abort-agent-scope.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in:
  - `sessions.abort` returns `no-active-run` and stale persisted `running` row is healed.
  - Restart recovery includes stale `running` rows without pre-set `abortedLastRun` marker.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:
  - Directly exercises the two affected state-transition seams with minimal unrelated runtime noise.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Sessions stuck in stale `running` state are now automatically cleared on `sessions.abort` no-active-run and at startup recovery, reducing indefinite message queue stalls.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[sessions.abort -> no active run] -> [row stays running] -> [lane starves]

After:
[sessions.abort -> no active run] -> [stale running row healed] -> [lane proceeds]

Startup recovery:
Before: running + abortedLastRun!=true -> skipped
After:  running (markerless stale) -> marked + recovered
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`No`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS (Darwin x64)
- Runtime/container: OpenClaw gateway + Node.js test runtime
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Gateway server methods + agent recovery tests
- Relevant config (redacted): default local config; proof replay used isolated dev runtime state

### Steps

1. Seed stale `running` session row with no active run.
2. Invoke `sessions.abort` with that key.
3. Verify before/after row state.
4. Run regression tests and targeted lint.

### Expected

- No-active-run branch heals stale persisted `running` row.
- Restart recovery handles markerless stale `running` rows.

### Actual

- Matches expected; live proof and targeted tests passed.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing behavior before + healed behavior after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios:
  - no-active-run abort stale-row healing path
  - non-running row remains untouched
  - markerless stale running row recovered at restart
- Edge cases checked:
  - no-op when row not running
  - existing active-run abort behavior preserved
- What you did **not** verify:
  - full production-like multi-channel crash injection E2E

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: accidentally clearing a genuinely active session row.
  - Mitigation: reconciliation is guarded by no-active-run abort outcome and conservative startup recovery flow; active-run paths remain unchanged.

target.title: fix(memory-core): treat dreaming fence marker lines as inside-fence in promotion guard (#80613)
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem.** `lineRangeOverlapsDreamingFence` in `extensions/memory-core/src/short-term-promotion.ts` tracked `insideFence` state from the fence marker lines but never flagged ranges that included the marker lines themselves. A relocated promotion range that ended on a `<!-- openclaw:dreaming:*:start -->` line, began on a `<!-- openclaw:dreaming:*:end -->` line, or covered only a marker line passed the guard, and the snippet built from those raw lines carried the marker text into `MEMORY.md`.
- **Why it matters.** The guard's contract is to refuse rehydrated ranges that overlap managed dreaming fences so dream artifacts cannot be promoted. Treating the marker lines themselves as outside-the-fence leaves a narrow but real leak: any window the relocator picks that clips a marker produces a snippet that includes the literal `<!-- openclaw:dreaming:*:start/end -->` text, and (when the window straddles content on either side of the marker) the adjacent line. This is the residual edge case noted by ClawSweeper on #80702 ("the managed-block leak half is related but should be handled by a focused memory-core promotion fix"), narrowed to the guard itself rather than a parallel sanitizer.
- **What changed.** `extensions/memory-core/src/short-term-promotion.ts`: marker lines are now checked against the range bounds; if a marker is inside the relocated range, the range is rejected. The pre-existing inside-fence tracking remains so interior lines between a start/end pair are still flagged. The contract elsewhere — the contamination predicate, the relocator, the apply path, the `MEMORY.md` writer — is untouched.
- **What did not change (scope boundary).** No change to `relocateCandidateRange`, `isContaminatedDreamingSnippet`, `applyShortTermPromotions`, `buildDailySnippetChunks`, `stripManagedDailyDreamingLines`, the CJK dedupe path (the second sub-bug of #80613 is being addressed separately by #80620), or any `MEMORY.md` rendering. No new sanitizer is introduced — this is a narrowing of the existing guard.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Refs #80613 (does not fully close; the dramatic full-Candidate leak from the issue body is already prevented by the merged fence-overlap guard in #68774. This PR narrows a residual marker-line edge case in the same guard.)
- Related: #80620 (CJK dedupe half, separate PR), #80702 (closed sanitizer approach, intentionally not duplicated here).
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: `lineRangeOverlapsDreamingFence` now flags relocated promotion ranges that include a dreaming fence marker line, preventing the marker text (and any adjacent fenced content captured by the same raw-line window) from being promoted into `MEMORY.md`.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS (Apple Silicon, Node v22.14.0, pnpm 11.1.0). The fix is mechanical and exercised by Vitest unit tests against the helper directly.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `pnpm test extensions/memory-core/src/short-term-promotion.test.ts -t "lineRangeOverlapsDreamingFence"` — targeted run of the affected describe block.
  - `pnpm test extensions/memory-core/src/short-term-promotion.test.ts` — full file regression.
  - `pnpm test extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.test.ts` — adjacent file regression.
  - `pnpm format:check extensions/memory-core/src/short-term-promotion.ts extensions/memory-core/src/short-term-promotion.test.ts` — format on changed files.
  - `pnpm lint extensions/memory-core/src/short-term-promotion.ts extensions/memory-core/src/short-term-promotion.test.ts` — lint on changed files.
  - `pnpm tsgo:extensions` and `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test` — typecheck.
- Evidence after fix:
  - Before the source change, the four added tests reproduce the gap (output captured locally):
    ```
    Tests  4 failed | 4 passed | 50 skipped (58)
    × returns true when the range ends on a Light Sleep start marker
    × returns true when the range begins on a Light Sleep end marker
    × returns true when the range covers only a marker line
    × returns true for REM marker single-line ranges even with no body between markers
    ```
    Each failure form: `expected false to be true`, confirming that the pre-patch guard returns `false` for a range that includes only a marker line.
  - After the source change:
    ```
    Tests  8 passed | 50 skipped (58)            # lineRangeOverlapsDreamingFence -t filter
    Tests  58 passed (58)                         # full short-term-promotion.test.ts
    Tests  41 passed (41)                         # full dreaming-phases.test.ts
    ```
  - Format/lint/typecheck on changed files: all clean. (Repo-wide format reports 34 pre-existing issues on `main` unrelated to this PR.)
- Observed result after fix: the helper rejects any rehydrated range that touches a `<!-- openclaw:dreaming:*:start -->` or `<!-- openclaw:dreaming:*:end -->` marker line. No regressions in the existing `lineRangeOverlapsDreamingFence` describe block or the broader test files.
- What was not tested: An end-to-end repro via `applyShortTermPromotions` that drives `relocateCandidateRange` to organically pick a marker-line-bordering window. Probe attempts on current `main` could not construct an organic trigger — the relocator's compare/quality scoring rarely prefers such windows when cleaner alternatives exist in the file — so the demonstration is at the unit level. The repo-wide format/lint/build/CI suite was not run locally; the changed files pass all relevant lanes individually.

target.title: gateway: gate tool invoke until startup hooks are ready
target.body:
## Summary
- gate tools.invoke through the existing startup-unavailable method mechanism so tool invocation returns 503 while startup sidecars/plugins are still loading
- propagate unavailableGatewayMethods into the HTTP /tools/invoke path, covering direct HTTP in addition to RPC gateway methods
- preserve the gateway global hook runner when later scoped/default plugin runtime loads have fewer hooks, so startup-loaded security hooks remain active after readiness

## Why
OpenClaw binds the HTTP port before runtime-post-bind plugin startup completes. Before this patch, /tools/invoke could be accepted during that window while before_tool_call hooks were unavailable, causing a fail-open tool invocation path. This exists as a startup race, not a steady-state plugin failure.

## Verification
- pnpm test src/gateway/tools-invoke-http.test.ts src/gateway/server-http.request-trace.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods.control-plane-rate-limit.test.ts src/plugins/loader.test.ts src/plugins/providers.test.ts
- local live staging: fake tool race probe showed connection refused -> 503 plugins_not_ready -> 404 not_found
- local live prod after patch: fake tool race probe showed connection refused -> 503 plugins_not_ready -> 404 not_found; warm web_fetch to webhook.site returned 403 [T-EXFIL-003]

## Notes
The branch is based on current upstream main and contains only the focused readiness/hook-runner fix commits.

target.title: Discord: add reaction notification wake policy
target.body:
## Summary

- Add shared `notificationWake.reactions` policy support so notification-backed reaction events queue by default, can opt into immediate wake, or can be disabled with `off`.
- Wire Discord reaction notifications through that shared helper after existing Discord ingress authorization and routing.
- Preserve stable `reaction_key` values for standard/custom emoji and keep deduped reaction events from waking.
- Add heartbeat preflight handling so `notification-wake:*` wakes still run when `HEARTBEAT.md` is empty, while generic notification events continue to honor the empty-file skip.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #83164
- Related #47687
- Related #83611
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Discord reaction notifications now pass through a shared notification wake policy. Accepted reactions enqueue routed system events by default, `notificationWake.reactions: "wake"` requests a targeted `notification-wake:discord-reaction` heartbeat only after successful enqueue, `off` skips enqueue after ingress authorization, and deduped events do not wake.
- Real environment tested: redacted OpenClaw source checkout on PR head `31de50ea3b8e`. Earlier redacted Discord API canary proof on this PR verified the Discord bot/API reaction path and listener event shape without publishing secrets, usernames, chat titles, local paths, or raw IDs; the current head was reverified with focused policy/runtime regression tests and project checks below.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/notification-system-events.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor.test.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.returns-default-unset.test.ts`
  - `pnpm config:channels:check`
  - `pnpm config:docs:check`
  - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md docs/channels/discord.md extensions/discord/src/monitor.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/listeners.reactions.ts src/config/types.agent-defaults.ts src/config/types.agents.ts src/config/types.channels.ts src/config/types.discord.ts src/config/types.notifications.ts src/config/types.openclaw.ts src/config/zod-schema.agent-defaults.ts src/config/zod-schema.agent-runtime.ts src/config/zod-schema.channels-config.ts src/config/zod-schema.notifications.ts src/config/zod-schema.providers-core.ts src/config/zod-schema.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.returns-default-unset.test.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.ts src/infra/notification-system-events.test.ts src/infra/notification-system-events.ts src/plugin-sdk/system-event-runtime.ts`
  - `git diff --check && git diff --cached --check`
  - `pnpm tsgo:prod`
  - `pnpm test:contracts:plugins`
- Evidence after fix:
  Redacted terminal output from the live Discord API canary for this PR's reaction listener path:

```json
{
  "discordBotIdentity": "verified_with_getCurrentUser",
  "canaryMessagePosted": true,
  "botReactionAddedViaRest": true,
  "guildHash": "01738befea7f",
  "channelHash": "5e33a98adfea",
  "messageHash": "9464114d77b7",
  "actorHash": "ab848dd6c34a",
  "systemEventQueued": true,
  "heartbeatRequestCallPresent": true,
  "sessionKeyHash": "7fbbcb1140b0",
  "eventTextShape": "Discord reaction added: <emoji> by <actor> on <guild> <channel> msg <message-id> (reaction_key=emoji:<emoji>) from <message-author>",
  "contextKeyHash": "d99c9ca6122b",
  "forceSenderIsOwnerFalse": true,
  "listenerLogs": []
}
```

  - Focused regression tests passed: 3 files, 92 tests.
  - Generated channel config check passed.
  - Generated docs baseline check passed with `OK docs/.generated/config-baseline.sha256`.
  - Formatter reported all 21 touched files use the correct format.
  - Git whitespace checks exited cleanly.
  - Typecheck passed for core and extensions through `pnpm tsgo:prod`.
  - Plugin contracts passed: 62 files, 857 tests.
- Observed result after fix: default Discord reaction handling queues exactly one system event and does not request heartbeat; opt-in `notificationWake.reactions: "wake"` requests `source: "notifications-event"`, `intent: "immediate"`, `reason: "notification-wake:discord-reaction"`, the routed `agentId`, and routed `sessionKey`; `off` skips enqueue; duplicate enqueue does not wake; custom emoji retain stable `reaction_key=custom_emoji:<id>` context keys; `notification-wake:*` preflight bypasses empty `HEARTBEAT.md` skip while generic notification events do not.
- What was not tested: no production Discord Gateway restart was performed after this follow-up refactor. The prior redacted live Discord API canary on this PR covered external bot/API reaction reachability; this update adds exact current-head local runtime proof for the wake policy and heartbeat preflight behavior.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): the previous patch woke every accepted Discord reaction immediately, which made the behavior too aggressive and inconsistent with the shared notification policy in #83611.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Discord reaction notification enqueue was wired directly to heartbeat wake behavior instead of going through a configurable notification wake policy.
- Missing detection / guardrail: tests covered reaction enqueue/routing but did not distinguish queue-by-default, opt-in wake, disabled enqueue, deduped no-wake, or empty-`HEARTBEAT.md` notification wake preflight.
- Contributing context (if known): Discord and Telegram reaction PRs were converging on similar semantics without a shared helper.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target tests or files:
  - `src/infra/notification-system-events.test.ts`
  - `extensions/discord/src/monitor.test.ts`
  - `src/infra/heartbeat-runner.returns-default-unset.test.ts`
- Scenario the tests lock in: shared policy resolution precedence, queue default, explicit wake, explicit off, deduped no-wake, Discord listener wiring, custom emoji stable keys, and notification wake preflight through empty `HEARTBEAT.md`.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: these tests exercise the shared helper and Discord listener seam directly without broad channel startup or live gateway flake.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): existing Discord reaction tests covered authorization/routing and enqueue shape; this PR adds the missing wake-policy assertions.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Discord reaction notifications that pass existing access controls are queued on the routed session by default. Operators can set `channels.discord.notificationWake.reactions: "wake"` for immediate reaction-only turns or `"off"` to suppress reaction notification enqueueing after authorization. System event text keeps stable `reaction_key=emoji:<emoji>` and `reaction_key=custom_emoji:<id>` markers.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Default:
[Discord reaction] -> [existing ingress authorization] -> [routed system event queued]

Opt-in wake:
[Discord reaction] -> [existing ingress authorization] -> [routed system event queued] -> [notification-wake heartbeat]

Disabled:
[Discord reaction] -> [existing ingress authorization] -> [notificationWake.reactions=off] -> [skip enqueue]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: redacted local development environment
- Runtime/container: local source checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Discord monitor harness and shared notification/heartbeat runtime tests
- Relevant config (redacted): default reaction policy, `notificationWake.reactions: "wake"`, and `notificationWake.reactions: "off"`

### Steps

1. Run the focused notification, Discord monitor, and heartbeat preflight tests.
2. Run generated config/docs checks.
3. Run formatter, whitespace, typecheck, and plugin contract checks.

### Expected

- Accepted reactions queue by default.
- Explicit wake policy requests the routed notification heartbeat after enqueue.
- Disabled policy skips enqueue.
- Deduped events do not wake.
- Empty `HEARTBEAT.md` does not suppress `notification-wake:*` turns.

### Actual

- Passed as described in the proof section.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: shared policy default/override resolution, Discord queue default, opt-in wake, off skip, deduped no-wake, custom emoji stable key, heartbeat preflight for notification wake, generated config/docs consistency, typecheck, plugin contract suite.
- Edge cases checked: blocked DM/guild/group reactions remain blocked by existing tests, reaction notification modes still gate behavior, generic notification events still skip when `HEARTBEAT.md` is empty.
- What you did **not** verify: production Discord Gateway delivery after this follow-up refactor.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? Optional new `notificationWake.reactions` config
- Migration needed? No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: operators expecting every reaction to wake immediately need an explicit opt-in.
  - Mitigation: default queue preserves visibility without surprise model wakes; `notificationWake.reactions: "wake"` restores immediate processing intentionally.
- Risk: notification wake preflight could over-bypass empty `HEARTBEAT.md` gating.
  - Mitigation: the bypass is limited to `source: "notifications-event"` plus `reason` prefix `notification-wake:`; generic notification events still skip.

target.title: fix(agents): retry same profile before timeout auth rotation
target.body:
## Summary

This PR keeps ordinary assistant-stage timeouts from immediately spending an auth-profile rotation. A first ordinary timeout now gets one bounded retry on the same model/profile; if the retry budget is exhausted, the existing auth-profile rotation path still runs.

The change deliberately does **not** weaken real failover for rate-limit/auth/quota/billing-style failures. Those reasons continue through the existing profile rotation / escalation path rather than being hidden by the timeout retry.

## What Problem This Solves

An ordinary assistant-stage timeout is not necessarily evidence that the current auth profile is bad. Rotating the auth profile on the first ordinary timeout can spend a scarce profile/fallback lane too early and make transient timeout handling look like an auth-profile failure.

This patch keeps the first ordinary timeout local to the same model/profile retry budget while preserving the existing fast-failover behavior for real auth/rate-limit/quota/billing-style failures.

## Why this is not covered by adjacent timeout fixes

Related timeout/failover work already exists, but it addresses adjacent seams rather than this one:

- #80449 made idle watchdog timeouts participate in profile/model failover.
- #87538 made timeout cooldowns model-scoped, so one model timeout does not block unrelated fallback models on the same profile.
- #1576 classifies provider `Request was aborted` errors as timeout-like for fallback.
- #95650 is about compaction retry behavior with backup auth profiles.
- #87122 is about provider overload cooldown semantics.

Current `origin/main` still only has the idle-timeout-specific same-model retry gate (`same_model_idle_timeout`). It does not have an ordinary request-timeout same-profile retry gate before `advanceAuthProfile()`.

## Behavior addressed

Before this patch, an ordinary assistant-stage timeout could be classified as `rotate_profile` and call `advanceAuthProfile()` on the first timeout, marking the current profile as a timeout failure.

After this patch:

- ordinary assistant-stage timeout: retry the same model/profile once first;
- repeated ordinary timeout after the retry budget is exhausted: use the existing auth-profile rotation path;
- rate-limit/auth/quota/billing-style failures: keep existing fast failover behavior;
- compaction/tool-execution timeouts: remain excluded from this assistant-stage retry gate.
- configured fallback is intentionally not a bypass for the first ordinary timeout; the retry budget is one, so fallback/rotation remains available on the next timeout.

## Implementation notes

- Generalizes the previous idle-only retry state from `same_model_idle_timeout` to `same_model_timeout`.
- Keeps the retry budget bounded at one same-profile retry.
- Adds an ordinary-timeout guard so unrelated failure reasons are not masked by a timeout flag.
- Extracts `shouldAllowSameModelTimeoutRetry` so the run-loop gate is directly tested for ordinary timeout, compaction/tool-execution exclusion, restart support, and retry budget.
- Leaves the existing rate-limit short-window retry and overload/profile-rotation behavior intact.

## Real Behavior Proof

### Runtime proof: local OpenAI-compatible timeout service

I re-ran this PR through the actual embedded runtime, not only the helper. The proof uses:

- current PR head: `1c3ee148bc44df0572a445cdeef6417cb409f013`
- `openclaw agent --local` from this PR checkout
- a temporary isolated `HOME`, OpenClaw config, state directory, and workspace
- a local OpenAI-compatible Responses service with its endpoint/port redacted
- synthetic model names: `timeout-primary` and `timeout-fallback`
- no live gateway, no production state, no external provider call, and no real credentials

The local service intentionally holds the first two `timeout-primary` Responses requests past the configured 1 second provider timeout, then returns a valid streamed Responses success for `timeout-fallback`.

Observed runtime request order:

```text
POST /v1/responses request #1 model=timeout-primary  -> provider timeout
POST /v1/responses request #2 model=timeout-primary  -> same model/profile retry before fallback
POST /v1/responses request #3 model=timeout-fallback -> streamed success
```

Observed final visible payload:

```text
PR95676_RUNTIME_OK
```

Redacted runtime log excerpts:

```text
[agent/embedded] [llm-timeout] openai/timeout-primary hit idle watchdog; retrying same profile before auth-profile rotation
[agent/embedded] embedded run failover decision: stage=assistant decision=fallback_model reason=timeout from=openai/timeout-primary profile=- rawError=LLM idle timeout (1s): no response from model
[model-fallback/decision] model fallback decision: decision=candidate_failed requested=openai/timeout-primary candidate=openai/timeout-primary reason=timeout next=openai/timeout-fallback detail=LLM idle timeout (1s): no response from model
[model-fallback/decision] model fallback decision: decision=candidate_succeeded requested=openai/timeout-primary candidate=openai/timeout-fallback reason=unknown next=none
[agent] run ended with stopReason=stop
```

Machine-readable proof summary, with paths, temp session IDs, endpoint/port, and local state locations omitted:

```json
{
  "proof": "PR #95676 embedded runtime timeout failover proof",
  "runtime": "openclaw agent --local",
  "service": "local OpenAI-compatible Responses timeout service",
  "head": "1c3ee148bc44df0572a445cdeef6417cb409f013",
  "assertion": {
    "ok": true,
    "responseModels": ["timeout-primary", "timeout-primary", "timeout-fallback"],
    "expected": ["timeout-primary", "timeout-primary", "timeout-fallback"],
    "visibleResponse": "PR95676_RUNTIME_OK"
  },
  "privacy": "temporary paths, endpoint/port, state locations, and credentials redacted; no live gateway or external provider used"
}
```

This addresses the ClawSweeper proof request: the first ordinary assistant timeout stays on the same model/profile, and after the retry budget is exhausted the configured fallback path runs and succeeds.

## Evidence

Current head: `1c3ee148bc44df0572a445cdeef6417cb409f013`.
Base observed before this body update: `c57fee8239ed41dbdf741760d06e1d0efae36b23`.

Local validation on the current checkout:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-embedded-agent.config.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.cross-provider-fallback-error-context.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/assistant-failover.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/failover-policy.test.ts test/scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.test.ts --maxWorkers=1 --no-fileParallelism
./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/assistant-failover.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/assistant-failover.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/idle-timeout-breaker.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.cross-provider-fallback-error-context.test.ts scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs test/scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.test.ts
./node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/assistant-failover.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/assistant-failover.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/idle-timeout-breaker.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.cross-provider-fallback-error-context.test.ts scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs test/scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.test.ts
git diff --check
```

Observed completed results on this pushed head:

```text
run.cross-provider-fallback-error-context.test.ts: 4 passed
failover-policy.test.ts + plugin-sdk-surface-report.test.ts: 36 passed
assistant-failover.test.ts: 28 passed
oxfmt focused files: passed
oxlint focused files: passed
git diff --check: passed
runtime proof with local OpenAI-compatible timeout service: passed
```

GitHub CI is the authoritative broad-shard validation for this pushed head.

## What was not tested

- No live gateway/production runtime validation.
- No external provider call.
- No real credential or production auth-profile material was used.
