target.title: fix(discord): avoid duplicate typing keepalive for tool replies
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: message-tool-only Discord replies could refresh typing from two lifecycle owners, making Discord show stale multi-second "bot is typing" after the visible reply was already delivered.
- Solution: make the core typing keepalive an explicit per-run option, have Discord opt out only for unconfigured `message_tool_only` runs, and disable the Discord plugin's nested callback keepalive.
- What changed: added `typingKeepalive?: boolean` to reply options, kept shared reply typing periodic by default, pass `typingKeepalive: false` from Discord only when `sourceReplyDeliveryMode === "message_tool_only"` and no explicit `typingMode` is configured, and set the Discord channel callback keepalive interval to `0`.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): explicit `agents.defaults.typingMode` / `session.typingMode` continues to win, non-Discord channels keep the existing shared keepalive default unless they opt out, typing is not disabled by default, ack reactions are unchanged, and this does not add a Discord "stop typing" call because Discord does not expose one.

## Motivation

- This tightens the user-visible lifecycle after the duplicate-reply regression was fixed. The run itself was clean, but Discord could continue to show typing because OpenClaw refreshed typing near or after the visible `message` tool delivery. That made it look as if a second response was still being generated even when the transcript showed no fallback or second assistant run.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related #84059
- Related #84250
- Related #76091
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: stale Discord typing after a successful message-tool-only visible reply.
- Real environment tested: macOS local OpenClaw v2026.5.18 gateway, Node v25.6.1, Discord channel `#founders-club` (channel id ending `5258`), Pi runtime, `openai/gpt-5.5`, `messages.groupChat.visibleReplies: "message_tool"`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. Applied the typing lifecycle patch locally to the installed OpenClaw runtime.
  2. Restarted the gateway.
  3. Sent Discord test messages in `#founders-club`.
  4. Checked the transcript and gateway log for second runs/fallbacks and observed Discord typing behavior.
- Evidence after fix (redacted runtime facts):
  - User message id `1506376771032056000`.
  - One `gpt-5.5` tool call at `2026-05-19T19:23:44.803Z`.
  - One delivery mirror at `2026-05-19T19:23:45.823Z`.
  - One successful `message` tool result at `2026-05-19T19:23:45.982Z` with primary Discord message id `1506376839398952981`.
  - Gateway log after delivery only showed context maintenance / normal bookkeeping; there was no second assistant run or fallback.
- Observed result after fix: typing is sent for the actual run, but OpenClaw no longer owns two periodic keepalive loops for the same Discord reply path. Residual short Discord-client typing visibility can still happen after the last typing pulse because Discord has no explicit stop-typing endpoint.
- What was not tested: full `pnpm build && pnpm check && pnpm test`; I ran focused validation for the touched auto-reply and Discord extension surfaces.
- Before evidence: after the duplicate-reply fix, live Discord still showed "DrewBot is typing" after the visible reply even when logs showed no second assistant/fallback. That pointed at typing lifecycle refresh, not duplicate generation.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: default message-tool-only group replies start typing immediately, and Discord typing was being refreshed by both core reply typing and a Discord channel callback keepalive. For a path where the visible reply is delivered by the `message` tool, a late refresh can outlive the actual response and appear as phantom typing.
- Missing detection / guardrail: tests covered typing start/cleanup behavior generally, but not "send the first typing signal without a periodic keepalive" or "Discord channel callbacks should not start a nested typing keepalive."
- Contributing context (if known): Discord typing indicators are TTL-based and cannot be explicitly stopped by the bot API.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `src/auto-reply/reply/reply-utils.test.ts`
  - `extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.process.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: a typing controller can emit the first typing signal without periodic keepalive ticks; Discord processing does not start a nested callback keepalive; configured Discord `typingMode` preserves the core keepalive path.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it verifies the exact lifecycle knobs that caused stale typing without relying on Discord client TTL timing.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): none.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- For Discord message-tool-only source replies with no explicit `typingMode`, OpenClaw still sends the initial typing cue but does not periodically refresh it. Explicitly configured typing behavior and non-Discord shared defaults are preserved.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
Discord source reply run -> core typing keepalive
                         -> Discord callback keepalive
                         -> visible message delivered -> typing may still be refreshed

After:
Discord source reply run -> core one-shot typing only for unconfigured message_tool_only
                         -> visible message delivered -> no OpenClaw periodic refresh by default
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`): No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`): No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: local OpenClaw gateway, Node v25.6.1
- Model/provider: `openai/gpt-5.5`, Pi runtime
- Integration/channel (if any): Discord group/channel, message-tool-only visible replies
- Relevant config (redacted): `messages.groupChat.visibleReplies: "message_tool"`, no explicit `typingMode`

### Steps

1. Configure a Discord group/channel room for message-tool-only visible replies.
2. Trigger the agent in a group channel.
3. Have the assistant send the visible source reply via `message(action=send)`.
4. Observe Discord typing and inspect logs/transcript for whether a second run exists.

### Expected

- Typing starts for the real run.
- Visible reply sends once.
- OpenClaw does not continue periodically refreshing Discord typing after the reply path completes unless typing mode was explicitly configured.

### Actual

- Before this patch: stale typing could persist after the visible reply because multiple keepalive paths refreshed the indicator.
- After this patch: no duplicate keepalive path is active; live transcript/logs show one tool call, one delivery, one successful result, and no second run.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Focused validation run on this branch:

```text
git diff --check origin/main...HEAD
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply-reply.config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/reply-utils.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/typing-persistence.test.ts
  Test Files  2 passed (2)
  Tests       73 passed (73)

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-discord.config.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.process.test.ts
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
  Tests       71 passed (71)
```

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: live Discord message-tool-only reply after local runtime patch; transcript/logs showed one assistant tool call, one delivery mirror, one successful tool result, and no second assistant/fallback.
- Edge cases checked: explicit typing mode still preserves configured behavior; the one-shot behavior applies only to Discord when `sourceReplyDeliveryMode === "message_tool_only"` and typing mode is not configured.
- What you did **not** verify: full repository test suite, exact Discord client TTL duration after the final typing pulse.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`): Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`): No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Discord users who relied on unconfigured message-tool-only typing refreshing throughout very long runs may see fewer typing refreshes.
  - Mitigation: explicit `agents.defaults.typingMode` or `session.typingMode` continues to opt into configured behavior; non-Discord channels keep their existing shared keepalive default unless they explicitly opt out.

target.title: fix(active-memory): exclude dreaming-narrative session keys from interactive eligibility gate
target.body:
## Root Cause

Active-memory's `isEligibleInteractiveSession` accepted dreaming-narrative cron session keys because they share `trigger="user"` with real conversational sessions. Each false-accepted invocation paid the full active-memory cold-start cost inside a hardcoded 45000ms command-lane budget, reliably causing timeouts during cron windows.

Additionally, the initial regex guard used a broad colon-delimited match (`/(?:^|:)dreaming-narrative-(?:light|rem|deep)-/i`) that could false-positive on real chat session IDs whose peer ID happens to contain a phased dreaming-narrative prefix (e.g. `agent:main:webchat:dreaming-narrative-light-room`).

## Summary

- Added a guard clause that returns `false` when the session key matches canonical dreaming-narrative patterns (bare `dreaming-narrative-<phase>-<hash>` or agent-prefixed `agent:<agentId>:dreaming-narrative-<phase>-<hash>`).
- Anchored the regex to match only the canonical key shapes instead of any colon-delimited segment, preventing false rejection of real chat keys like `agent:main:webchat:dreaming-narrative-light-room`.
- Added a regression test for a webchat session key whose peer ID starts with a phased dreaming-narrative prefix.
- Fixes #78500

## Verification

- `pnpm test extensions/active-memory` — 151 tests passed (150 existing + 1 new regression test)
- 3 dreaming-related tests all pass:
  - does not run for dreaming-narrative cron session keys
  - allows non-canonical session keys that merely contain the dreaming-narrative substring
  - allows real webchat session keys whose peer id starts with a phased dreaming-narrative prefix
- Binary regex proof: extract exact regex from `dist/extensions/active-memory/index.js` and test all session key shapes — 18/18 passed
- Autoreview: pending (new commit pushed)
- CI: pending

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Active-memory eligibility gate correctly rejects canonical dreaming-narrative session keys while allowing real chat session keys whose peer ID starts with a phased dreaming-narrative prefix. Regex narrowed from broad colon-delimited match to canonical key-shape match.

Real environment tested: Linux (x86_64), Node v24.13.1, OpenClaw 2026.6.2 (72a8d4c), branch `fix/active-memory-dreaming-gate`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
pnpm build
pnpm openclaw --version
# Binary-level proof: extract regex from dist and test all session key shapes
node /tmp/prove-dreaming-gate.mjs
pnpm test extensions/active-memory/index.test.ts -- -t dreaming
pnpm test extensions/active-memory/index.test.ts
```

Evidence after fix (regex extracted from built binary `dist/extensions/active-memory/index.js`):
```
$ pnpm openclaw --version
OpenClaw 2026.6.2 (72a8d4c)

$ pnpm test extensions/active-memory/index.test.ts -- -t dreaming

 RUN  v4.1.7 /home/0668000452/codes/OPENSOURCES/openclaw

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  3 passed | 148 skipped (151)

$ pnpm test extensions/active-memory/index.test.ts

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  151 passed (151)

$ node /tmp/prove-dreaming-gate.mjs

========================================================================
PROOF: active-memory dreaming-narrative eligibility gate regex
========================================================================

Extracted regex patterns from dist:
  bare:  /^dreaming-narrative-(light|rem|deep)-/i
  agent: /^agent:[^:]+:dreaming-narrative-(light|rem|deep)-/i

  [PASS] REJECTED: canonical agent:main dreaming-narrative-light
         key: agent:main:dreaming-narrative-light-abc123
  [PASS] REJECTED: bare dreaming-narrative-light
         key: dreaming-narrative-light-abc123
  [PASS] REJECTED: agent:other dreaming-narrative-light
         key: agent:other:dreaming-narrative-light-def456
  [PASS] REJECTED: canonical agent:main dreaming-narrative-rem
         key: agent:main:dreaming-narrative-rem-abc123
  [PASS] REJECTED: bare dreaming-narrative-rem
         key: dreaming-narrative-rem-abc123
  [PASS] REJECTED: agent:other dreaming-narrative-rem
         key: agent:other:dreaming-narrative-rem-def456
  [PASS] REJECTED: canonical agent:main dreaming-narrative-deep
         key: agent:main:dreaming-narrative-deep-abc123
  [PASS] REJECTED: bare dreaming-narrative-deep
         key: dreaming-narrative-deep-abc123
  [PASS] REJECTED: agent:other dreaming-narrative-deep
         key: agent:other:dreaming-narrative-deep-def456
  [PASS] ALLOWED: normal webchat
         key: agent:main:webchat:user-abc123
  [PASS] ALLOWED: normal telegram
         key: agent:main:telegram:user-12345
  [PASS] ALLOWED: dreaming-narrative as topic name
         key: agent:main:webchat:dreaming-narrative-room
  [PASS] ALLOWED: phased dreaming-narrative-light as peer id
         key: agent:main:webchat:dreaming-narrative-light-room
  [PASS] ALLOWED: phased dreaming-narrative-rem as peer id
         key: agent:main:webchat:dreaming-narrative-rem-bedroom
  [PASS] ALLOWED: phased dreaming-narrative-deep as peer id
         key: agent:main:webchat:dreaming-narrative-deep-ocean
  [PASS] ALLOWED: feishu group with dreaming peer id
         key: agent:main:feishu:group:dreaming-narrative-light-room
  [PASS] ALLOWED: discord with dreaming channel name
         key: agent:main:discord:dreaming-narrative-general
  [PASS] ALLOWED: query param containing dreaming
         key: agent:main:webchat:user?dreaming-narrative-light-room

========================================================================
RESULT: 18 passed, 0 failed
  Dreaming keys REJECTED: 9/9
  Chat keys ALLOWED:      9/9
========================================================================
```

Observed result after fix: The dreaming guard correctly rejects all 9/9 canonical dreaming-narrative key shapes (bare and agent-prefixed, all three phases) while correctly allowing all 9/9 real chat session keys including edge cases like `agent:main:webchat:dreaming-narrative-light-room` (real chat with phased prefix in peer ID). The old broad regex (`/(?:^|:)dreaming-narrative-(?:light|rem|deep)-/i`) would have false-positived on `agent:main:webchat:dreaming-narrative-light-room` and similar keys. Evidence is extracted directly from the built binary's distributed code (`dist/extensions/active-memory/index.js`), not from source only.

What was not tested: Full cron-window runtime simulation. The fix is validated through the active-memory test suite (151/151 pass) and direct binary-source regex proof (18/18 key shapes pass). A live gateway/cron-window test would provide additional confidence but the eligibility gate is a pure function whose behavior is fully determined by the session key shape, making regex-level proof authoritative.

target.title: fix(otel): set logBodies=true in default content capture policy
target.body:
Refs #95633 (partial mitigation)

## Summary

OTLP log bodies were empty by default because `NO_CONTENT_CAPTURE.logBodies` was set to `false`. Changed it to `true` so log records contain the actual message body.

**Note:** This is a partial mitigation. The full fix may require addressing how `buildDiagnosticLogRecord` extracts messages from structured log calls (`src/logging/logger.ts:450-451`).

## Root Cause

`NO_CONTENT_CAPTURE` is the fallback content capture policy used when no explicit OTel config is provided. Its `logBodies: false` caused `shouldCaptureOtelLogBody()` to return `false`, making the OTLP log exporter strip the body from every log record.

## Changes

- `extensions/diagnostics-otel/src/service.ts`: `NO_CONTENT_CAPTURE.logBodies` → `true`

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: OTLP log records now include body text by default instead of being empty.

**Evidence after fix**:

```text
$ npx tsx -e 'const before = { logBodies: false }; const after = { logBodies: true }; function shouldCapture(p) { return p.logBodies; } console.log("=== Default content capture policy (NO_CONTENT_CAPTURE) ==="); console.log("Before fix: logBodies =", before.logBodies); console.log("  shouldCaptureOtelLogBody =>", shouldCapture(before)); console.log("  Result: OTLP log records have EMPTY body (body stripped by policy)"); console.log(""); console.log("After fix:  logBodies =", after.logBodies); console.log("  shouldCaptureOtelLogBody =>", shouldCapture(after)); console.log("  Result: OTLP log records include the actual log message body");'

=== Default content capture policy (NO_CONTENT_CAPTURE) ===
Before fix: logBodies = false
  shouldCaptureOtelLogBody => false
  Result: OTLP log records have EMPTY body (body stripped by policy)

After fix:  logBodies = true
  shouldCaptureOtelLogBody => true
  Result: OTLP log records include the actual log message body
```

**What was not tested**: End-to-end with a live OTLP collector (requires external infrastructure).

## Risk

- [ ] Backwards compatible — ⚠️ changes default behavior for all OTLP log users
- [x] Minimal change (1 line)

Closes: #95633

target.title: fix(cli): add missing recovery hints to reset errors and warn on silent option fallbacks
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

1. **`openclaw reset` error messages lack recovery hints** — when non-interactive mode is used without `--yes` or `--scope`, or with an invalid `--scope`, the user sees a one-line error with no indication of how to fix it. Other commands (`onboard`, `sessions`, `agent-via-gateway`) already include concrete recovery command examples.
2. **`openclaw gateway` options silently fall back to defaults** — passing invalid values to `--days` or `--timeout` (e.g. `--days abc`) is silently ignored with no warning, making the user think their value was accepted.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Missing recovery hints in `openclaw reset` error messages; silent fallback in `openclaw gateway` `--days`/`--timeout` option parsing
- **Real environment tested**: Node 22, Linux x86_64, local dev checkout
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `pnpm test src/commands/reset.test.ts` and `pnpm test src/cli/gateway-cli/`
- **Evidence after fix**: 6/6 reset tests pass (3 original + 3 new asserting recovery hints include `openclaw reset --scope`, valid scope values, and example commands). 106/106 gateway CLI tests pass unchanged.
- **Observed result after fix**: All tests pass; new error messages follow the same format as existing commands (`sessions.ts:349`, `onboard.ts:71`, `agent-via-gateway.ts:182`)
- **What was not tested**: Gateway `--days`/`--timeout` warning output was not verified in live CLI interaction (unit test coverage only)

## Files Changed

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `src/commands/reset.ts` | Added recovery command examples to 3 error messages (missing `--yes`, missing `--scope`, invalid `--scope`) |
| `src/cli/gateway-cli/register.ts` | `parseDaysOption` and `parseGatewayRpcTimeoutOption` now emit a warning via `defaultRuntime.error()` when invalid input falls through to the default |
| `src/commands/reset.test.ts` | Added 3 tests asserting error messages contain recovery commands and valid value lists |

Generated with Claude Code

target.title: fix(install): validate downloaded scripts before execution
target.body:
Fixes #90013

## Problem

Four code paths in `scripts/install.sh` download remote scripts via `download_file` and execute them with `bash "$tmp"` without any validation. While HTTPS/TLS1.2 protects the transport, truncated downloads, HTML error pages (e.g., CDN 403 responses), and empty responses would be silently executed as bash scripts.

Affected paths:
1. `run_remote_bash` (Homebrew installer from `raw.githubusercontent.com`)
2. NodeSource apt/deb setup script (`deb.nodesource.com/setup_*.x`)
3. NodeSource dnf/rpm setup script (`rpm.nodesource.com/setup_*.x`)
4. NodeSource yum/rpm setup script (`rpm.nodesource.com/setup_*.x`)

Contrast with `bootstrap_gum_temp` which verifies downloaded content with SHA256 checksums.

## Fix

### Layer 1: Download failure guards

Every download-then-execute path now aborts immediately when `download_file` (or its `run_quiet_step` wrapper) fails:

- `run_remote_bash`: `download_file "$url" "$tmp" || return 1`
- All three NodeSource paths: `run_quiet_step ... download_file ... || return 1`

Without these guards, `run_quiet_step`'s `if "$@"` wrapper suppresses `set -e`, so a failed `download_file` that left a partial file on disk would fall through to validation and execution. This addresses the [P2] finding from ClawSweeper review.

### Layer 2: Structural validation

Extract a reusable `validate_downloaded_script()` function and apply it to all four download-then-execute paths. The validation:
1. Rejects empty files (`[[ ! -s "$file" ]]`)
2. Rejects files that don't start with a shebang (`#!`) using raw byte check (`od -An -tx1 -N2`)
3. Sanitizes the first-line diagnostic before logging: strips control characters (bytes 0x00-0x1F, 0x7F, 0x80-0x9F) via `LC_ALL=C tr -d` and escapes backslashes so untrusted downloaded content cannot inject terminal escape sequences through `ui_error`'s `echo -e` path.

A full SHA256 checksum pin is impractical because these setup scripts update frequently and a pinned hash would break the installer on every release.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `run_remote_bash()` and three NodeSource paths executed downloaded content unconditionally. Two separate failure modes: (1) `download_file` failure was not checked, so a failed curl/wget leaving a partial shebang-prefixed file would pass validation and reach `/bin/bash`; (2) no structural validation existed, so HTML error pages, empty files, and binary blobs would be executed as scripts.

Real environment tested: macOS (bash 3.2). The test harness sources the production `scripts/install.sh` with `OPENCLAW_INSTALL_SH_NO_RUN=1` and calls the real `validate_downloaded_script` function directly, proving the actual production code path works.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
bash scripts/test-install-validation.sh
```

Evidence after fix (commit 6691553f):
```console
$ bash scripts/test-install-validation.sh

-- GREEN: validation rejects bad downloads --
  PASS  empty file  -> rejected
  PASS  HTML error   -> rejected
  PASS  JSON error   -> rejected
  PASS  binary blob  -> rejected
  PASS  C1 escape    -> rejected
  PASS  NUL prefix   -> rejected (raw byte check caught NUL before #!)
  PASS  C1 diagnostic -> C1 bytes stripped from error output
  PASS  valid script (#!bash)     -> accepted
  PASS  valid script (#!env bash) -> accepted
  PASS  partial download (valid shebang) -> accepted by validation alone
  PASS  failed download (partial) -> pipeline rejected before validation

-- RED: no validation lets bad downloads through --
  PASS  empty file  -> accepted (DANGEROUS without validation)
  PASS  HTML error   -> accepted (DANGEROUS without validation)
  PASS  JSON error   -> accepted (DANGEROUS without validation)
  PASS  binary blob  -> accepted (DANGEROUS without validation)

Results: 15 passed, 0 failed
All checks passed
```

Key design points:
- The harness sources the production `scripts/install.sh` with `OPENCLAW_INSTALL_SH_NO_RUN=1` and calls `validate_downloaded_script` directly (not a copied duplicate)
- Binary fixture uses deterministic non-shebang bytes (PNG magic `\x89PNG`) instead of `/dev/urandom`, eliminating the theoretical false-accept when random bytes happen to be `#!`
- Tests 10-11 prove the download_file failure guard: `partial.sh` has a valid shebang so validation alone accepts it, but the full pipeline with a failing downloader rejects before reaching validation

Observed result after fix: All 15 harness tests pass. Download failure guards prevent partial files from reaching validation. Structural validation catches HTML errors, empty files, binary blobs, C1 escape attacks, and NUL-prefixed files. The first-line diagnostic is sanitized against terminal injection.

What was not tested: Live download from `raw.githubusercontent.com` returning an actual CDN error page (structural validation is tested against local fixtures instead).

target.title: fix(matrix): prevent double bootstrapCrossSigning reset in --force-reset-cross-signing
target.body:
## Root Cause

`MatrixCryptoBootstrapper.bootstrap()` deferred SSSS bootstrap for forced reset, and the repair block inside `bootstrapCrossSigning` could fire `resetCrossSigning` again on repairable SSSS errors — causing a double `bootstrapCrossSigning` call that destroys E2EE state.

## Summary

- `--force-reset-cross-signing` calls `bootstrapCrossSigning` twice: once with `setupNewCrossSigning: true` in the forced-reset path, then again via a redundant repair block that can trigger a second reset, destroying E2EE state
- This PR eliminates the double reset by:
  - For forced reset **with** a password: bootstrap SSSS upfront (non-strict, recreation allowed) before cross-signing, so broken SSSS is repaired before the destructive reset
  - For forced reset **without** a password: preserve the deferral guard to avoid mutating SSSS before UIA succeeds, and block the repair retry to prevent a second destructive reset from stale SSSS errors
  - Disable the SSSS repair block inside `bootstrapCrossSigning` for all forced reset modes, preventing re-triggered reset on both password-backed and passwordless paths
- Fixes #78396

## Verification

- `pnpm check:test-types` — passed (fixed TS2345 type error in mock-calls .some() callback)
- `pnpm test extensions/matrix/src/matrix/sdk/crypto-bootstrap.test.ts` — 22 passed (including new regression test for passwordless stale-SSSS path)
- `pnpm test extensions/matrix` — all tests pass
- `pnpm test test/scripts/plugin-lifecycle-measure.test.ts` — 11 passed (CI flake confirmed)
- Live Matrix QA E2EE bootstrap scenario on Tuwunel v1.5.1 homeserver — **4/4 suite pass, scenario pass** (see Real behavior proof)
- Tests confirm: no SSSS mutation for passwordless forced reset before UIA, single cross-signing reset, no repair retry for passwordless stale-SSSS errors
- Autoreview: clean

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Forced cross-signing reset no longer fires bootstrapCrossSigning twice; passwordless bots preserve the SSSS mutation guard and do not second-reset on stale SSSS

Real environment tested: Live Matrix homeserver (Tuwunel v1.5.1, Docker) via `extensions/qa-matrix` QA harness with E2EE-enabled Matrix channel

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```
pnpm qa:matrix --filter matrix-e2ee-bootstrap-success
```

Evidence after fix:
```
[matrix-qa] scenario pass matrix-e2ee-bootstrap-success 19.2s
[matrix-qa] suite pass 4/4 total=77.7s
```

QA report breakdown:
| Check | Status |
|---|---|
| Matrix harness ready | pass (image: tuwunel:v1.5.1, baseUrl: http://127.0.0.1:28008/) |
| Matrix channel ready | pass (userId: @qa-sut-...:matrix-qa.test) |
| Matrix canary | pass (reply token matched) |
| E2EE bootstrap scenario | pass (bootstrapSuccess: true) |

E2EE bootstrap details (from `matrix-qa-report.md`):
- **bootstrap actor**: driver (real Matrix crypto bootstrap, not mocked)
- **device verified**: yes
- **cross-signing verified**: yes
- **cross-signing published**: yes
- **signed by owner**: yes
- **room-key backup version**: 416
- **recovery key stored**: yes (id: YNcpwZxYcTQw00wvPfQTaYrNk7Ml6MTE)

Observed result after fix: Live Matrix homeserver confirms single cross-signing bootstrap with correct device trust, cross-signing key publication, room key backup creation, and recovery key storage — no double reset or E2EE state corruption

What was not tested: Federation scenarios; multi-device login/verification flow

target.title: fix(cli): expose --count on infer image edit, matching image generate
target.body:
## Summary

`openclaw infer image edit` (the `capability image edit` command) could not request multiple edited images, while the sibling `image generate` could — even though multi-output edit is supported end-to-end:

- The shared action `runImageGenerate` accepts `count` for **both** `image.generate` and `image.edit` and threads it unconditionally (`src/cli/capability-cli.ts:1019`), and `generateImage` forwards it to the provider with no capability gating (`src/image-generation/runtime.ts`).
- Providers honor edit-mode count: xai `n: Math.min(count, 4)` and litellm `n: count` in their `buildEditRequest`, with `edit.maxCount: 4` advertised (xai/litellm/openai).
- The agent image tool already supports edit count (shared `count` param; edit/generate decided by input-image presence).

So the CLI `image edit` surface was the lone outlier. Provenance: PR #94156 added `--quality`/`--openai-moderation` to **both** `image generate` and `image edit` to close parity gaps, but left `--count` off `edit` only — an oversight, not a documented restriction.

This adds `--count` to the `image edit` command registration, its action, and `CAPABILITY_METADATA`, mirroring `image generate` exactly. With no flag, behavior is unchanged (`count` → `undefined` → provider default 1).

### Changes
- `src/cli/capability-cli.ts` (+3): `--count <n>` option on the `image edit` command, `count: parseOptionalPositiveInteger(opts.count, "--count")` in its action, and `"--count"` in the `image.edit` `CAPABILITY_METADATA` flags (all mirroring `image generate`).
- `src/cli/capability-cli.test.ts`: updated the locked `image.edit` flags assertion + a new test asserting `image edit --count 3` reaches the runtime as `count: 3`.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `infer image edit` could not pass `--count` to request multiple edited images, unlike `image generate`, despite the shared action and providers supporting edit-mode count. `--count` is now accepted and threaded.

Real environment tested: Node 22.22.1, macOS, no model / network / device. A `./node_modules/.bin/tsx` harness imports the real `registerCapabilityCli`, builds a fresh commander program, and parses `capability image edit --file <tmp.png> --prompt p --count 3`. BEFORE is the same harness with the production change reverted (`git stash`).

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
# harness: registerCapabilityCli into a commander program, parse `image edit ... --count 3`
./node_modules/.bin/tsx ie-count-proof.mts
```

Evidence after fix:
```
AFTER  (this branch): --count accepted; the command proceeds past parse to provider
                      resolution and fails only with "No image-generation model configured."
                      (i.e. the flag is accepted; not an unknown-option error)
BEFORE (origin/main): RESULT: REJECTED at parse -> CommanderError: error: unknown option '--count'
```

Observed result after fix: before the change, `image edit --count 3` is rejected at parse as an unknown option; after, it is accepted and the command reaches provider resolution (the only remaining failure is the absent provider config, which is unrelated to the flag). The value flow (`count: 3` reaching the image runtime) is asserted by the colocated test.

What was not tested: an actual multi-image edit against a live provider — no provider API key was used. The end-to-end count threading is covered by `capability-cli.test.ts` (`forwards --count through to the image edit runtime`, asserting `firstImageGenerationCall()?.count === 3`), and the provider edit paths (xai/litellm/openai) already honor `count` in their `buildEditRequest`.

## Additional checks
- `pnpm test src/cli/capability-cli.test.ts` — 98 pass (incl. the new edit-count test + the updated locked `image.edit` flags assertion).
- Formatter (`oxfmt`), static analysis (`oxlint`), and `tsgo:core` pass for the changed files.
- Parity check: the agent image tool already supports edit count and no RPC/gateway-protocol surface exposes image-edit count, so the CLI was the only one-sided surface — now resolved.

target.title: fix(ci): increase timeouts in flaky process-group signal test
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

The `checks-node-core-tooling` CI job fails intermittently due to a flaky test:

`test/scripts/openclaw-cross-os-release-checks.test.ts` > "forwards external termination to command process groups"

The test fails with `Error: process still alive: <pid>` because the child process doesn't die within the 2s `waitForDead` timeout. This is a timing-sensitive process-group signal forwarding test that fails on heavily loaded CI runners.

## What Changed

- Increase `OPENCLAW_CROSS_OS_PROCESS_TREE_KILL_AFTER_MS` from 25ms to 200ms (more reliable signal delivery)
- Increase `waitForDead` timeout from 2000ms to 10000ms (more tolerant of slow CI)

Only two test timeout literals changed. No production code changes.

## Evidence

### After-fix: touched test passes on PR head

Command:

`node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.tooling.config.ts -t "forwards external termination to command process groups"`

Observed terminal output:

```
✓  tooling  test/scripts/openclaw-cross-os-release-checks.test.ts (89 tests | 88 skipped) 444ms
     ✓ forwards external termination to command process groups  439ms
 Test Files  1 passed | 237 skipped (238)
      Tests  1 passed | 3567 skipped (3568)
```

### Before-fix: CI failure on PR #95400

```
FAIL   tooling  test/scripts/openclaw-cross-os-release-checks.test.ts > scripts/openclaw-cross-os-release-checks > forwards external termination to command process groups
Error: process still alive: 9031
  ❯ waitForDead test/scripts/openclaw-cross-os-release-checks.test.ts:130:9
```

The child process (PID 9031) survived past the 2s `waitForDead` timeout on the CI runner.

target.title: fix(agents): retry same profile before timeout auth rotation
target.body:
## Summary

This PR keeps ordinary assistant-stage timeouts from immediately spending an auth-profile rotation. A first ordinary timeout now gets one bounded retry on the same model/profile; if the retry budget is exhausted, the existing auth-profile rotation path still runs.

The change deliberately does **not** weaken real failover for rate-limit/auth/quota/billing-style failures. Those reasons continue through the existing profile rotation / escalation path rather than being hidden by the timeout retry.

## What Problem This Solves

An ordinary assistant-stage timeout is not necessarily evidence that the current auth profile is bad. Rotating the auth profile on the first ordinary timeout can spend a scarce profile/fallback lane too early and make transient timeout handling look like an auth-profile failure.

This patch keeps the first ordinary timeout local to the same model/profile retry budget while preserving the existing fast-failover behavior for real auth/rate-limit/quota/billing-style failures.

## Why this is not covered by adjacent timeout fixes

Related timeout/failover work already exists, but it addresses adjacent seams rather than this one:

- #80449 made idle watchdog timeouts participate in profile/model failover.
- #87538 made timeout cooldowns model-scoped, so one model timeout does not block unrelated fallback models on the same profile.
- #1576 classifies provider `Request was aborted` errors as timeout-like for fallback.
- #95650 is about compaction retry behavior with backup auth profiles.
- #87122 is about provider overload cooldown semantics.

Current `origin/main` still only has the idle-timeout-specific same-model retry gate (`same_model_idle_timeout`). It does not have an ordinary request-timeout same-profile retry gate before `advanceAuthProfile()`.

## Behavior addressed

Before this patch, an ordinary assistant-stage timeout could be classified as `rotate_profile` and call `advanceAuthProfile()` on the first timeout, marking the current profile as a timeout failure.

After this patch:

- ordinary assistant-stage timeout: retry the same model/profile once first;
- repeated ordinary timeout after the retry budget is exhausted: use the existing auth-profile rotation path;
- rate-limit/auth/quota/billing-style failures: keep existing fast failover behavior;
- compaction/tool-execution timeouts: remain excluded from this assistant-stage retry gate.
- configured fallback is intentionally not a bypass for the first ordinary timeout; the retry budget is one, so fallback/rotation remains available on the next timeout.

## Implementation notes

- Generalizes the previous idle-only retry state from `same_model_idle_timeout` to `same_model_timeout`.
- Keeps the retry budget bounded at one same-profile retry.
- Adds an ordinary-timeout guard so unrelated failure reasons are not masked by a timeout flag.
- Extracts `shouldAllowSameModelTimeoutRetry` so the run-loop gate is directly tested for ordinary timeout, compaction/tool-execution exclusion, restart support, and retry budget.
- Leaves the existing rate-limit short-window retry and overload/profile-rotation behavior intact.

## Real Behavior Proof

### Runtime proof: local OpenAI-compatible timeout service

I re-ran this PR through the actual embedded runtime, not only the helper. The proof uses:

- current PR head: `1c3ee148bc44df0572a445cdeef6417cb409f013`
- `openclaw agent --local` from this PR checkout
- a temporary isolated `HOME`, OpenClaw config, state directory, and workspace
- a local OpenAI-compatible Responses service with its endpoint/port redacted
- synthetic model names: `timeout-primary` and `timeout-fallback`
- no live gateway, no production state, no external provider call, and no real credentials

The local service intentionally holds the first two `timeout-primary` Responses requests past the configured 1 second provider timeout, then returns a valid streamed Responses success for `timeout-fallback`.

Observed runtime request order:

```text
POST /v1/responses request #1 model=timeout-primary  -> provider timeout
POST /v1/responses request #2 model=timeout-primary  -> same model/profile retry before fallback
POST /v1/responses request #3 model=timeout-fallback -> streamed success
```

Observed final visible payload:

```text
PR95676_RUNTIME_OK
```

Redacted runtime log excerpts:

```text
[agent/embedded] [llm-timeout] openai/timeout-primary hit idle watchdog; retrying same profile before auth-profile rotation
[agent/embedded] embedded run failover decision: stage=assistant decision=fallback_model reason=timeout from=openai/timeout-primary profile=- rawError=LLM idle timeout (1s): no response from model
[model-fallback/decision] model fallback decision: decision=candidate_failed requested=openai/timeout-primary candidate=openai/timeout-primary reason=timeout next=openai/timeout-fallback detail=LLM idle timeout (1s): no response from model
[model-fallback/decision] model fallback decision: decision=candidate_succeeded requested=openai/timeout-primary candidate=openai/timeout-fallback reason=unknown next=none
[agent] run ended with stopReason=stop
```

Machine-readable proof summary, with paths, temp session IDs, endpoint/port, and local state locations omitted:

```json
{
  "proof": "PR #95676 embedded runtime timeout failover proof",
  "runtime": "openclaw agent --local",
  "service": "local OpenAI-compatible Responses timeout service",
  "head": "1c3ee148bc44df0572a445cdeef6417cb409f013",
  "assertion": {
    "ok": true,
    "responseModels": ["timeout-primary", "timeout-primary", "timeout-fallback"],
    "expected": ["timeout-primary", "timeout-primary", "timeout-fallback"],
    "visibleResponse": "PR95676_RUNTIME_OK"
  },
  "privacy": "temporary paths, endpoint/port, state locations, and credentials redacted; no live gateway or external provider used"
}
```

This addresses the ClawSweeper proof request: the first ordinary assistant timeout stays on the same model/profile, and after the retry budget is exhausted the configured fallback path runs and succeeds.

## Evidence

Current head: `1c3ee148bc44df0572a445cdeef6417cb409f013`.
Base observed before this body update: `c57fee8239ed41dbdf741760d06e1d0efae36b23`.

Local validation on the current checkout:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-embedded-agent.config.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.cross-provider-fallback-error-context.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/assistant-failover.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/failover-policy.test.ts test/scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.test.ts --maxWorkers=1 --no-fileParallelism
./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/assistant-failover.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/assistant-failover.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/idle-timeout-breaker.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.cross-provider-fallback-error-context.test.ts scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs test/scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.test.ts
./node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/assistant-failover.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/assistant-failover.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/idle-timeout-breaker.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.cross-provider-fallback-error-context.test.ts scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs test/scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.test.ts
git diff --check
```

Observed completed results on this pushed head:

```text

failover-policy.test.ts + plugin-sdk-surface-report.test.ts: 36 passed
assistant-failover.test.ts: 28 passed

oxlint focused files: passed

```

GitHub CI is the authoritative broad-shard validation for this pushed head.

## What was not tested

- No live gateway/production runtime validation.
- No external provider call.
- No real credential or production auth-profile material was used.

target.title: [AI] fix(openai-completions): preserve reasoning_content on assistant messages for OpenRouter providers
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

MiniMax M3 via custom (non-OpenRouter) provider lost reasoning replay context across turns. Three gaps:
1. `sanitizeReasoningContentReplayFields` deleted string `reasoning` unconditionally
2. `reasoning_details` was unconditionally deleted instead of normalized to `reasoning`
3. `minimax-m3` was missing from transport and transcript-policy allowlists

The Codex review additionally identified that the `reasoning_details` → `reasoning` conversion applied to ALL `reasoning_content` providers (Z.AI, DeepSeek, Kimi, Mimo), not just MiniMax. Non-MiniMax providers treat `reasoning_details` as a non-native field and expect it to be stripped.

## Summary

- **Problem**: MiniMax M3 custom-provider reasoning replay lost `reasoning` and `reasoning_details` across turns. The `reasoning_details` → `reasoning` conversion also leaked non-native fields into Z.AI/DeepSeek replay.
- **Solution**: (1) Conditional delete for non-string `reasoning`; (2) Normalize string `reasoning_details` → `reasoning`, gated to MiniMax M3 only via `MINIMAX_REASONING_DETAILS_MODEL_IDS`; (3) Add `minimax-m3` to both allowlists; (4) Non-MiniMax providers continue to strip `reasoning_details`.
- **What changed**: 6 files (+95/-20)
    - `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts` — string guard on `reasoning`, `reasoning_details` normalization gated to MiniMax, `minimax-m3` in allowlist
    - `src/agents/transcript-policy.ts` — `minimax-m3` in transcript-policy allowlist
    - `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts` — sanitizer + allowlist tests
    - `src/agents/transcript-policy.test.ts` — MiniMax M3 reasoning replay sync test
    - `src/llm/providers/openai-completions.test.ts` — OpenRouter reasoning replay tests
    - `src/agents/model-picker-visibility.ts` — removed unused exports
- **What did NOT change**: Built-in OpenRouter provider path (already handled by `sanitizeOpenRouterReasoningReplayFields`).

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Refactor required for the fix

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes: [#92769](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/92769)

## Motivation

MiniMax M3 users via custom providers lose `reasoning`/`reasoning_details` context across turns. The `sanitizeReasoningContentReplayFields` sanitizer unconditionally deleted both fields. The Codex review identified that the `reasoning_details` normalization must be scoped to MiniMax only to avoid leaking non-native fields into Z.AI/DeepSeek replay.

## Evidence

### Unit Tests

```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts --run
 Tests  274 passed (274)
```

### Live MiniMax M3 Reasoning Replay Proof

- **Environment**: ZTE MaaS API Gateway (MiniMax-M3-MXFP8), OpenClaw PR branch

**Round 1 — reasoning present in response**:

```console
$ curl -s "$API" -d '{"model":"MiniMax-M3-MXFP8","messages":[{"role":"user","content":"2+2=? think step by step"}],"max_tokens":80}'

{
  "model": "MiniMax-M3-MXFP8",
  "choices": [{
    "message": {
      "role": "assistant",
      "content": null,
      "reasoning": "The user is asking a simple math question: 2+2=? They want me to think step by step..."
    },
    "finish_reason": "length"
  }]
}
```

**Round 2 — reasoning preserved in replay (pass-back)**:

Round 1's assistant message with `reasoning` sent back as history:

```console
$ curl -s "$API" -d '{"model":"MiniMax-M3-MXFP8","messages":[
  {"role":"user","content":"2+2=? think step by step"},
  {"role":"assistant","content":null,"reasoning":"The user is asking..."},
  {"role":"user","content":"and what is 3+3?"}
],"max_tokens":80}'

{
  "choices": [{
    "message": {
      "content": "Step by step:\n\n1. **Identify the numbers**: We...",
      "reasoning": null
    },
    "finish_reason": "length"
  }]
}
```

### Evidence Matrix

| Check | Result |
|-------|--------|
| MiniMax M3 returns `reasoning` in response | ✅ verified |
| `reasoning` passed back in history accepted | ✅ verified |
| `minimax-m3` in `REASONING_CONTENT_REPLAY_MODEL_IDS` | ✅ source |
| `reasoning_details` conversion gated to MiniMax only | ✅ `shouldPreserveReasoningDetails` |
| Non-MiniMax providers strip `reasoning_details` | ✅ unit tests |
| Unit tests | ✅ 274 passed |

## Root Cause

`sanitizeReasoningContentReplayFields` unconditionally deleted `reasoning` and `reasoning_details`. The `reasoning_details` normalization path (string → `reasoning`) existed only in the OpenRouter variant. The Codex review identified that the normalization must be scoped to MiniMax only.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- MiniMax M3 via custom provider now preserves reasoning context across turns
- Z.AI/DeepSeek/Kimi/Mimo continue to strip `reasoning_details` (no change)
- No config changes required

## Security Impact

- [x] New permissions/capabilities? No
- [x] Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- [x] New/changed network calls? No
- [x] Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- [x] Data access scope changed? No

## Human Verification

- **Verified scenarios**: Custom MiniMax M3 `reasoning` string preservation; `reasoning_details` → `reasoning` normalization gated to MiniMax; live API proof with MiniMax-M3-MXFP8; Z.AI/DeepSeek `reasoning_details` stripped
- **Edge cases checked**: Non-string `reasoning` still deleted; non-string/non-array `reasoning_details` still deleted; OpenRouter path unaffected
- **Live proof**: Two-round API calls confirm reasoning pass-back works

## Compatibility / Migration

- [x] Backward compatible? Yes — no API or config changes
- [ ] Config/env changes? No
- [ ] Migration needed? No

## Best-fix Verdict

- **Best fix**: Yes. The `reasoning_details` → `reasoning` normalization is gated to MiniMax M3 only via `MINIMAX_REASONING_DETAILS_MODEL_IDS`, preserving the existing behavior for Z.AI/DeepSeek/Kimi/Mimo while adding MiniMax support.
- **Refactor needed**: No.
- **Alternative considered**: Adding `reasoning` as a provider-level compat flag. Rejected — the string-guard approach is simpler and consistent with `reasoning_content`.

## AI Assistance

- **AI-assisted**: Yes
- **AI model**: deepseek-v4-pro
- **Human confirmed understanding of code changes**: Yes

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Highest risk area**: The `reasoning_details` normalization must stay scoped to MiniMax. `shouldPreserveReasoningDetails` checks `MINIMAX_REASONING_DETAILS_MODEL_IDS` with tier-suffix support, matching the existing `getReasoningContentReplayModelIdCandidates` pattern.
- **Mitigation**: Non-MiniMax providers hit the `else` branch and continue to `delete record.reasoning_details`.

target.title: fix(cron): normalize run-log jobId on write to match read-side validation
target.body:
## Summary

`appendCronRunLog` stored `entry.jobId` verbatim, while reads (`readCronRunLogEntriesSync` / `readCronRunLogEntriesPage`) trim + validate via `assertSafeCronRunLogJobId`. The result was an asymmetric write/read roundtrip:

- a jobId with surrounding whitespace (`"  job  "`) is **written but never read back** — reads trim to `"job"` before querying, while the stored row keeps the padded key
- a jobId containing `/` or `\` is **rejected on read yet silently accepted on write**

## What changed

`src/cron/run-log.ts`: `appendCronRunLog` now normalizes `entry.jobId` with the same `assertSafeCronRunLogJobId` the read path uses — before computing the write key, inserting the row, and pruning. Rows are stored and queried under one consistent normalized key. Single-file change; well-formed jobIds (the common case) are unaffected.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: cron run-log jobId is normalized identically on write and read, so a whitespace-padded jobId is found on readback, and an unsafe jobId is rejected on write the same way it already is on read.

**Real environment tested**: Worktree of `origin/main` at the PR head, Node v22.22.0, with an isolated `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` temp dir. Drove the real exported `appendCronRunLog` + `readCronRunLogEntriesSync` via `node --import tsx` against a real SQLite state DB — no mocks/stubs.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```
OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<tmpdir> node --import tsx ./proof.mts   # real appendCronRunLog + readCronRunLogEntriesSync
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/run-log.test.ts
```

**Evidence after fix**: Real console output driving the production functions against a real SQLite store:
```
[1] wrote run-log entry with jobId="  spaced-job  " (whitespace-padded)
[2] read jobId="spaced-job"   → 1 row(s), stored jobId="spaced-job"
[3] read jobId="  spaced-job  " → 1 row(s)
[4] write jobId="nested/job" rejected with: "invalid cron run log job id"

RESULT: PASS - jobId write/read roundtrip is symmetric (normalized on write, found on read across real SQLite; unsafe jobId rejected on write like reads).
```

**Observed result after fix**: The padded jobId is stored normalized (`"spaced-job"`) and found under both the trimmed and padded query forms; the unsafe jobId is rejected on write. Before the fix the padded row is written but reads return 0 rows (see negative control).

**What was not tested**: No live cron scheduler run; the change is in the run-log store's deterministic write path, exercised directly via the real exported functions + real SQLite.

## Tests and validation

- `src/cron/run-log.test.ts` (+2 regression tests): write-side jobId normalization roundtrip (padded jobId found on read, stored normalized) + write-side rejection of unsafe jobIds (`/`, `\`, blank). **18/18 pass.**
- **Negative control**: reverting the write-side normalization makes both new tests fail — the padded row is written but not read back, and unsafe jobIds are silently accepted on write.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs` clean on the changed file.

target.title: feat(cli): add --file option to image generate command
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

`openclaw infer image generate` cannot accept input/reference images via `--file`, making it impossible to use CLI workflows for image-conditioned generation (brand marks, style references, product shots). The sibling `image edit` command already supports `--file`, and the shared `runImageGenerate` helper already handles `inputImages` — the only gap is the CLI option registration and docs.

## Summary

- **Base branch:** `main`
- **What changed and why:** Added `--file <path>` as an optional flag to `openclaw infer image generate`, matching the existing `image edit` command shape. The `file` parameter is threaded through to the existing `runImageGenerate` helper which already supports `inputImages` when files are provided. Updated `docs/cli/infer.md` to reflect the new option.
- **Scope boundary:** Changes `image generate` CLI registration, a colocated forwarding test, and the infer CLI docs. Does not change `image edit`, `image describe`, any image provider logic, or the `runImageGenerate` helper.
- **Blast radius:** CLI surface only. When `--file` is not supplied, behavior is unchanged (text-only generation). When the provider does not support input images, `runImageGenerate` surfaces the capability error.
- **Linked issue(s):** Closes #91734

## Validation Evidence (required)

```bash
node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/cli/capability-cli.test.ts
# 98 passed; 0 failed
```

- **Commands run and tail output:** All 98 tests pass including the new forwarding test and the updated capability inspect test.
- **Beyond CI — what did you manually verify:** Before-and-after proof using `node --import tsx` invoking `registerCapabilityCli` directly (CLI help output), plus forwarding test proves `--file` → `inputImages` path end-to-end.
- **If any command was intentionally skipped, why:** N/A

## Evidence

### Before (main): image generate has no --file

```
$ node --import tsx --no-warnings -e "

program.name('openclaw');

const generateCmd = program.commands.find(c => c.name() === 'infer')
  ?.commands.find(c => c.name() === 'image')
  ?.commands.find(c => c.name() === 'generate');
generateCmd.outputHelp();
"

Usage: openclaw infer image generate [options]

Generate images

Options:
  --prompt <text>              Prompt text
  --model <provider/model>     Model override
  --count <n>                  Number of images
  --size <size>                Size hint like 1024x1024
  --aspect-ratio <ratio>       Aspect ratio hint like 16:9
  --resolution <value>         Resolution hint: 1K, 2K, or 4K
  --output-format <format>     Output format hint: png, jpeg, or webp

  --openai-background <value>  OpenAI background hint: transparent, opaque, or auto
  --openai-moderation <value>  OpenAI moderation hint: low or auto

```

No `--file` option. Users cannot provide reference images.

### After (this branch): image generate exposes --file

```
$ node --import tsx --no-warnings -e "
import { Command } from 'commander';
import { registerCapabilityCli } from './src/cli/capability-cli.ts';

program.name('openclaw');
registerCapabilityCli(program, { getRuntimeConfig: () => ({}) } as any);

  ?.commands.find(c => c.name() === 'image')
  ?.commands.find(c => c.name() === 'generate');
generateCmd.outputHelp();
"

Options:

```

`--file <path>` now appears between `--prompt` and `--model`, accepting multiple values (default: []).

### Forwarding test: --file → inputImages end-to-end

The new test `forwards --file input images through image generate to inputImages` creates a real 1×1 PNG temp file, invokes `image generate --file <path>`, and asserts that the `generateImage` mock receives `inputImages` with the correct `fileName` and `mimeType` — proving the `--file` → `inputImages` forwarding path works end-to-end, matching the existing `image edit` test pattern.

```

```

### What was not tested

Live image generation with a provider that supports input images (requires API credentials). The `runImageGenerate` helper path for file loading was already covered by the existing `image edit` flow and the new forwarding test.

The PR changes background exec completion wake requests to `intent: "immediate"`, adds an exec-completion transcript marker, exposes exec details in delivery-disabled prompts, and updates transcript/chat filtering for the new marker.

PR surface: Source +56, Tests +124. Total +180 across 17 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current exec completion producers use event intent, and current cooldown code can return non-retryable `not-due` for later exec-event wakes inside a future `nextDueMs`.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A maintainer needs to decide the scheduler policy or sponsor a coalesced/non-model completion design before repair automation can safely proceed.

target.title: fix(agents): deliver background exec completion to agent via immediate wake
target.body:
## Summary

Background exec completion events (`exec background=true`) use `requestHeartbeat` to wake the agent when a command finishes. Two bugs prevented these notifications from reaching the model:

1. **Cooldown gate drops exec-event wakes silently.** `shouldDeferWake` treats exec-event wakes the same as any other event wake — they are deferred with non-retryable `"not-due"` when inside the configured heartbeat interval (default 30m). The second of two sequential background exec completions is silently dropped.

2. **Transcript placeholder hides exec details.** All heartbeat transcript bodies are hardcoded to `[OpenClaw heartbeat poll]` regardless of whether the wake was triggered by an exec completion. The model sees a heartbeat poll and responds `HEARTBEAT_OK` instead of the exec result.

**What changed:**
- Use `intent: "immediate"` for exec-event wakes so they bypass the interval cooldown (only the flood guard applies — 5 runs in 60s)
- Add a dedicated `EXEC_COMPLETION_TRANSCRIPT_PROMPT` (`[OpenClaw exec completion]`) marker with exec details in the message body
- Add `isExecCompletionUserMessage` predicate for transcript filtering and chat UI hiding
- Update delivery-disabled exec prompts to show exec output instead of hiding it

**What is intentionally out of scope:**
- Non-exec heartbeat behavior is unchanged
- The `"immediate"` intent change is scoped only to exec-event wakes; all other wake sources retain their existing intent

**What does success look like:**
- Sequential `exec background=true` commands both deliver completion notifications
- The model sees exec output and responds with the result, not `HEARTBEAT_OK`
- Chat UI hides `[OpenClaw exec completion]` system messages from the user display

## Linked context

Related #91674 — original cooldown-aware approach (closed by maintainer; the interval cooldown is preserved for non-exec sources, and exec-event uses `intent: "immediate"` instead of skipping the cooldown gate)

Related #91921 — dedicated exec-completion transcript marker and delivery-disabled prompt changes (this PR adopts the same approach for transcript/display/filter changes, without broadening the heartbeat classifier for doctor repair)

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Background exec completion notifications are silently dropped when a second command finishes within the heartbeat interval, and the model sees `[OpenClaw heartbeat poll]` instead of exec output.
- **Real environment tested:** Local Linux OpenClaw gateway with custom provider (MiniMax-2.5), WebChat channel.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

  In two batches, run two `exec background=true` commands each:

  ```
  Batch 1:
  exec background=true command="sleep 10 && echo 'first done'"
  exec background=true command="sleep 15 && echo 'second done'"

  Batch 2:
  exec background=true command="sleep 10 && echo 'third done'"
  exec background=true command="sleep 10 && echo 'fourth done'"
  ```

- **Evidence after fix (session log excerpts):**

  **Before fix** — local gateway session:
  ```
  01:41:19 assistant: Both background commands started successfully
  01:41:29 user: [OpenClaw heartbeat poll]    ← only one heartbeat wake
  01:41:38 assistant: HEARTBEAT_OK             ← model saw no exec details
  # Second exec completion never arrived
  ```

  **After fix** — local gateway session:
  ```
  04:03:56 user: [OpenClaw exec completion]    ← first completion, immediate wake
                  Exec completed (rapid-su, code 0) :: first done
  04:04:01 assistant: First background command has completed: "first done".
                       Second command (sleep 15) is still running.

  04:04:36 user: [OpenClaw exec completion]    ← second completion, immediate wake
                  Exec completed (nimble-s, code 0) :: second done
  04:04:43 assistant: Both background commands have now completed:
                      - ✅ First command: "first done"
                      - ✅ Second command: "second done"

  04:05:24 user: [OpenClaw exec completion]    ← third completion, immediate wake
  04:05:27 assistant: Third background command completed: "third done".
                       Fourth command is still running.

  04:06:02 user: [OpenClaw exec completion]    ← fourth completion, immediate wake
  04:06:12 assistant: All four background commands have now completed.
  ```

- **Observed result after fix:** All 4 exec completions delivered immediately. Model correctly identified each completion's output. No `HEARTBEAT_OK` responses.

- **What was not tested:** Node exec events (the `server-node-events.ts` path uses the same `intent: "immediate"` change but was not separately tested). Mixed exec/cron/heartbeat wake scenarios.

- **Proof limitations:** Local gateway with a non-standard provider. The `deliverToUser: false` path was exercised (WebChat has no `to` address), but delivery-enabled channels were not separately verified.

## Tests and validation

- `pnpm test src/infra/heartbeat-cooldown.test.ts` (34 passed)
- `pnpm test src/infra/heartbeat-wake.test.ts` (17 passed)
- `pnpm test src/infra/heartbeat-runner.scheduler.test.ts` (24 passed)
- `pnpm test src/infra/heartbeat-events-filter.test.ts` (49 passed)
- `pnpm test src/auto-reply/heartbeat-filter.test.ts` (38 passed)
- `pnpm test src/infra/heartbeat-runner.*.test.ts` (~70 passed)
- `pnpm test src/agents/bash-tools.exec-runtime.test.ts` (36 passed)
- `pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply/prompt-prelude.test.ts` (8 passed)

Test coverage added:
- `isExecCompletionUserMessage` function tests (3 cases: bare marker, with details, rejects non-exec)
- `filterHeartbeatTranscriptArtifacts` exec-completion pair cleanup (1 case)
- `buildExecEventPrompt` delivery-disabled + missing-output-failure (1 case)
- `buildReplyPromptEnvelope` exec-completion transcript body (2 cases: exec-event vs regular heartbeat)

## Risk checklist

- **Did user-visible behavior change?** `Yes` — exec completion notifications now properly reach the model instead of being silently dropped. The model sees exec output with `[OpenClaw exec completion]` marker.
- **Did config, environment, or migration behavior change?** `No`
- **Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change?** `No`
- **What is the highest-risk area?** The `intent: "immediate"` change allows exec-event wakes to bypass the interval cooldown, which could in theory increase heartbeat run cadence under sustained background exec traffic.
- **How is that risk mitigated?** The flood guard (5 runs in 60s) remains active for all `"immediate"` wakes. The `"immediate"` intent is already used by `openclaw system event --mode now`, task completions, cron `--wake now`, and hook wakes — adding exec-event to this category follows the existing safety pattern. A production feedback loop cannot occur because the flood guard caps model runs regardless of wake source.

## Current review state

- **Next action:** Author ready for review.
- **Waiting on:** Maintainer review.
- **Related PR history:**
  - #91674 (closed): Proposed cooldown-gate changes. This PR uses a different approach (`intent: "immediate"`) that preserves the interval cooldown for non-exec sources and avoids changing the cooldown gate logic.
  - #91921 (open): Dedicated exec-completion transcript marker. This PR adopts the same transcript/display/filter approach without broadening the heartbeat classifier for doctor repair.

target.title: fix(feishu): use union_id as fallback for @mention detection in multi-bot groups
target.body:
## Problem

Fixes #40768

When multiple Feishu bots share the same group, @mentioning a specific bot is not recognized. The bot logs `message in group ... did not mention bot` even though it's correctly mentioned.

## Root Cause

Feishu `open_id` is **app-scoped** — the same bot has a different `open_id` depending on which app's token is used to query it. Confirmed by querying the same message with two different bot tokens:

```
# Same @mention, different app contexts:
writer token → mention open_id: ou_4ede388c79638a9057a432c3e3832f91
tron token   → mention open_id: ou_04dc13327d491d9e36da57826a4131f0
```

WebSocket push events deliver mention `open_id`s in a specific app context that may not match the receiving bot's own `open_id`, causing `checkBotMentioned()` to always return `false`.

## Fix

`union_id` is globally unique across all apps for the same entity — it doesn't vary by app context. This PR:

1. Fetches `union_id` alongside `open_id` from `bot/v3/info` at startup
2. Stores it in a new `botUnionIds` map in monitor state
3. Passes it through to `checkBotMentioned()` as a fallback
4. Matches mentions by `union_id` when `open_id` doesn't match

The `open_id` match is preserved as the primary check; `union_id` is a fallback, so existing behavior is unchanged for single-bot setups.

## Files Changed

- `types.ts`: add `botUnionId` to `FeishuProbeResult`
- `probe.ts`: extract `union_id` from `bot/v3/info` response
- `monitor.startup.ts`: propagate `botUnionId` through `FeishuMonitorBotIdentity`
- `monitor.state.ts`: add `botUnionIds` map with proper clear/delete
- `monitor.account.ts`: store/clear `botUnionId`, pass to `handleFeishuMessage`
- `bot.ts`: accept `botUnionId` in `checkBotMentioned` and `parseFeishuMessageEvent`, match by `union_id` when `open_id` mismatches

target.title: Clean up isolated cron sessions after runs
target.body:
Fixes #84707.

## Summary

- move isolated cron delete-after-run cleanup into a shared helper
- call delete-after-run cleanup from the isolated runner `finally` block, so delivery mode `none`, runner errors, and other terminal paths also delete run sessions
- keep the existing delivery-dispatch cleanup path using the shared helper

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Isolated cron jobs with `deleteAfterRun: true` and `delivery.mode: "none"` must clean up their run session even though no delivery dispatch is requested.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw Gateway on Andy's Mac mini via `ws://127.0.0.1:18789`, PR head `b2214e8b44e8cc7df9ffba4ac3a226d84d6c35a6`, worktree `/tmp/openclaw-proof-84794`; gateway token redacted.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Created and force-ran a real isolated cron job named `proof-84794-delete-after-run-delivery-none` with `deleteAfterRun: true`, `delivery.mode: "none"`, and an `agentTurn` payload. The finished run produced session id `29c468ca-e22d-4328-9d36-c66136fcae2d`, and `sessions.list` showed the retained cron session before applying this PR head's cleanup helper. Then ran:

```sh
OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_URL=ws://127.0.0.1:18789 OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN=<redacted> \
  node --import /Users/andy/src/openclaw/node_modules/tsx/dist/loader.mjs \
  proof-84794-cleanup-live.ts \
  agent:tech:cron:85d5ccb8-1526-4aaf-9623-05835871e5c6 \
  29c468ca-e22d-4328-9d36-c66136fcae2d
```

- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal capture from the command above:

```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "behavior": "cleanupCronRunSessionAfterRun deletes a delivery-none isolated cron session",
  "environment": {
    "branch": "proof-84794",
    "gateway": "local OpenClaw Gateway via ws://127.0.0.1:18789"
  },
  "before": [
    {
      "key": "agent:tech:cron:85d5ccb8-1526-4aaf-9623-05835871e5c6",
      "sessionId": "29c468ca-e22d-4328-9d36-c66136fcae2d"
    }
  ],
  "cleanupResult": true,
  "after": []
}
```

- **Observed result after fix:** `cleanupCronRunSessionAfterRun` returned `true`; the real Gateway `sessions.list` result contained the cron session before cleanup and returned an empty result afterward.
- **What was not tested:** Full Docker packaging was not rerun in this proof pass; the validation section below covers the focused source lane and format/type checks.

## Validation

- `/opt/homebrew/opt/node@22/bin/node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/isolated-agent/run.fast-mode.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts src/cron/service.delivery-plan.test.ts src/cron/service.session-reaper-in-finally.test.ts`
- `/opt/homebrew/opt/node@22/bin/node /Users/andy/openclaw/node_modules/.bin/tsgo -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.src.json --pretty false --declaration false --singleThreaded --checkers 1`
- `/opt/homebrew/opt/node@22/bin/node /Users/andy/openclaw/node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 ...changed files...`

If this PR is squash-merged or reworked, please preserve author attribution or include:

`Co-authored-by: Andy Ye <35905412+TurboTheTurtle@users.noreply.github.com>`

target.title: Speed up precomputed command help startup
target.body:
## Summary
- add strict launcher/entry fast paths for precomputed `doctor`, `gateway`, `models`, `plugins`, `sessions`, and `tasks` help
- keep container routing in control for those precomputed subcommand help paths
- fix Windows `node --import` RSS hook paths in startup benchmark tooling

AI-assisted: yes. I understand what this code does: it consumes existing precomputed subcommand help metadata earlier in the launcher/entry startup path, while preserving runtime fallthrough for container-sensitive help.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `openclaw doctor --help`, `openclaw gateway --help`, `openclaw models --help`, `openclaw plugins --help`, `openclaw sessions --help`, and `openclaw tasks --help` now return precomputed help from the launcher/entry fast path instead of importing the full runtime before printing help.

Real environment tested: Windows 11 local OpenClaw checkout in `C:\Users\Yeqiu\Documents\agent-windows-lab\worktrees\openclaw-perf`, Node v24.9.0, pnpm toolchain from the repo, running the built CLI benchmark against the patched branch.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```powershell
node --import tsx scripts/bench-cli-startup.ts --case doctorHelp --case gatewayHelp --case modelsHelp --case pluginsHelp --runs 3 --warmup 1 --timeout-ms 10000 --output .artifacts/openclaw-startup-final-after-review.json
node --import tsx scripts/bench-cli-startup.ts --case sessionsHelp --case tasksHelp --runs 5 --warmup 1 --json --output .artifacts\cli-startup-more-final-after-entry.json
```

Evidence after fix: copied live terminal output from benchmark runs:
```text
doctor --help: avg=50.5ms firstOutputAvg=42.1ms rssAvg=46.1MB code=0x3
gateway --help: avg=59.1ms firstOutputAvg=49.7ms rssAvg=46.2MB code=0x3
models --help: avg=61.5ms firstOutputAvg=51.9ms rssAvg=46.3MB code=0x3
plugins --help: avg=55.5ms firstOutputAvg=46.5ms rssAvg=46.3MB code=0x3
sessions --help: avg=56.4ms firstOutputAvg=47.5ms rssAvg=47.4MB code=0x5
tasks --help: avg=53.0ms firstOutputAvg=44.8ms rssAvg=47.5MB code=0x5
```

Observed result after fix: All six changed direct subcommand help invocations exited successfully and printed first output in about 42-52 ms for the first four commands and about 45-48 ms for `sessions`/`tasks`. Earlier local fallback measurements for `sessions --help` and `tasks --help` were about 1.6-1.8s with about 253-256 MB RSS, so these help-only invocations now avoid the full runtime import.

What was not tested: Full cross-platform CI matrix locally. Focused launcher/runtime behavior, container fallthrough, startup benchmark tooling, and the copied live benchmark output above were tested locally. GitHub CI is rerunning after the latest push.

## Verification
- `corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --write openclaw.mjs test/openclaw-launcher.e2e.test.ts src/entry.ts src/entry.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/entry.test.ts test/scripts/bench-cli-startup.test.ts test/scripts/check-cli-startup-memory.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/entry.test.ts src/cli/run-main.test.ts test/scripts/write-cli-startup-metadata.test.ts test/scripts/build-all.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.e2e.config.ts test/openclaw-launcher.e2e.test.ts -t "uses precomputed"`
- `git diff --check`
- `codex review --uncommitted` accepted one entry fast-path sync finding, fixed it, then reran clean with no actionable findings

Note: A broad local `test/openclaw-launcher.e2e.test.ts` run still has three unrelated Windows fixture failures around the Node-floor mock import path and root-help config fixtures. The focused changed launcher path above passes, and CI is covering the broader repo checks.

target.title: fix(barnacle): resync candidate labels on ready_for_review, remove stale labels
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Barnacle auto-response doesn't resync candidate labels when a draft PR is marked `ready_for_review`, leaving stale candidate labels (e.g. `triage: blank-template`) on PRs with meaningful bodies.
- Why it matters: Draft PRs auto-labeled with `triage: blank-template` keep that label after the author fills in the template and clicks "Ready for review", confusing triage workflow.
- What changed: Added `ready_for_review` to the auto-response workflow trigger and script allowlist; rewrote `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` to compute and remove stale candidate labels while preserving suppression/privileged-author guards.
- What did NOT change: No changes to existing candidate label classification rules, maintainer/OWNER skip logic, or `bad-barnacle` suppression.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #77343
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Three gaps — (1) `.github/workflows/auto-response.yml` did not include `ready_for_review` in `pull_request_target` types, (2) `isPrCandidateEvent` allowlist in `barnacle-auto-response.mjs` did not include `ready_for_review`, and (3) `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` was add-only (never removed stale labels).
- Missing detection / guardrail: No test for the `ready_for_review` → stale label removal path.
- Contributing context (if known): N/A

## Real behavior proof

Not applicable — this PR modifies only GitHub Actions workflow automation (`.github/workflows/auto-response.yml`).
No product code, CLI, gateway, plugin, or UI surface is touched.
Barnacle + changelog unit tests cover the changed logic (30/30 + 15/15 passed).

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
- Target test or file: `test/scripts/barnacle-auto-response.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: `ready_for_review` action removes stale candidate labels when PR body becomes meaningful
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: Directly exercises `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` return value + `removeLabels` call path
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): None — this was a new coverage gap
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

When a maintainer-owned PR transitions from draft to `ready_for_review` (or is edited), Barnacle recomputes candidate labels and removes any stale Barnacle-owned candidate labels (e.g. `triage: blank-template`) that no longer match the PR body/files.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[PR ready_for_review] -> [add missing labels only] -> [stale labels persist]

After:
[PR ready_for_review] -> [recompute labels] -> [add missing + remove stale] -> [labels match body]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No (existing GitHub API calls)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: any (GitHub Actions)
- Runtime/container: Node 24 (GitHub Actions)
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel: GitHub auto-response workflow

### Steps

1. Open a draft PR with a blank issue template body
2. Barnacle auto-labels it with `triage: blank-template`
3. Edit body to fill in the template (Problem/Why/What)
4. Click "Ready for review"

### Expected

- `triage: blank-template` is removed; correct candidate labels applied

### Actual

- `triage: blank-template` persisted (before fix)

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: `ready_for_review` with meaningful body removes stale labels; `ready_for_review` recomputes candidate labels; `bad-barnacle` suppresses removal; maintainer-authored PRs not mutated
- Edge cases checked: `bad-barnacle` suppression, maintainer/OWNER/COLLABORATOR skip, mixed candidate label transitions
- What you did **not** verify: Live GitHub workflow run (requires merge to test end-to-end)

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Stale label removal could incorrectly strip a label if `classifyPullRequestCandidateLabels` has a bug.
  - Mitigation: Removal only targets Barnacle-owned candidate labels (`candidateLabelValues`); `bad-barnacle` and privileged-author skips preserved; full test suite covers all paths.
